function.name: simCallMethod
function.description: Calls a method

param.name: target
param.description: target object

param.name: name
param.description: method name

param.name: inputStack
param.description: input stack handle. The input stack holds the input arguments. Can be -1

param.name: outputStack
param.description: output stack handle. The output stack holds the return arguments. Can be -1

param.name: detachedScript
param.description: the handle of a detached script, that will be used if the method is implemented in Lua. Can be -1, which targets the sandbox script

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error, otherwise a stack handle.

function.name: simCreateBuffer
function.description: Creates a buffer. The buffer has to be released with simReleaseBuffer

param.name: size
param.description: size of the buffer in bytes

param.name: return
param.description: buffer if operation was successful, null otherwise

function.name: simReleaseBuffer
function.description: Releases a buffer previously created by CoppeliaSim

param.name: buffer
param.description: buffer to be released

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetLastError
function.description: Retrieves and clears the last generated API call error or warning message

param.name: return
param.description:
Error or warning message buffer or null if no error message is present. A warning starts with
warning@
. The user has to delete the returned buffer with a call to simReleaseBuffer

function.name: simSetLastError
function.description: Sets a custom error message and possibly raises a script error

param.name: setToNull
param.description: set to null

param.name: errorMessage
param.description: error message

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simCopyStack
function.description: Duplicates a stack object

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: a stack handle obtained with simCreateStack

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error, otherwise the handle of the duplicated stack.

function.name: simCreateStack
function.description: Creates a stack object that is mainly used to exchange complex data in an easy way with scripts

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error, otherwise a stack handle.

function.name: simDebugStack
function.description: Prints the content of the specified stack to the console

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: a stack handle obtained with simCreateStack

param.name: cIndex
param.description: the zero-based index of the stack location to print, or -1 to print the full stack.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error.

function.name: simGetStackBoolValue
function.description: Tries to retrieve the value at the top of the stack, if that value is a bool

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: a stack handle obtained with simCreateStack

param.name: boolValue
param.description: a pointer to a location receiving the bool value.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error, 0 if the value is not a bool, 1 otherwise.

function.name: simGetStackDoubleTable
function.description: Retrieves a double-precision double array from an array-type table at the top of the stack. Table values that are not numbers are converted to 0.0

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: a stack handle obtained with simCreateStack

param.name: array
param.description: a pointer to a location receiving the double values. Use simGetStackTableInfo to determine the number of values the table contains.

param.name: count
param.description: the size of the array. If the array is bigger than the table, it will be padded with 0.0.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error, 0 if item is not an array or does not contain only numbers, 1 otherwise.

function.name: simGetStackDoubleValue
function.description: Tries to retrieve the value at the top of the stack, if that value is a number

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: a stack handle obtained with simCreateStack

param.name: numberValue
param.description: a pointer to a location receiving the double value.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error, 0 if the value is not a number, 1 otherwise.

function.description: Retrieves a float array from an array-type table at the top of the stack. Table values that are not numbers are converted to 0.0

param.name: array
param.description: a pointer to a location receiving the float values. Use simGetStackTableInfo to determine the number of values the table contains.

param.name: count
param.description: the size of the array. If the array is bigger than the table, it will be padded with 0.0.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error, 0 if item is not an array or does not contain only numbers, 1 otherwise.

function.name: simGetStackItemType
function.description: Returns the type of a specific stack item

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: a stack handle obtained with simCreateStack

param.name: cIndex
param.description: the zero-based index of the stack item. Negative values indicate reverse counting from the top of the stack (i.e. -1 indicates the stack top)

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error, otherwise the stack item type

function.name: simGetStackStringType
function.description: Returns the type of a specific stack string

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: a stack handle obtained with simCreateStack

param.name: cIndex
param.description: the zero-based index of the stack string. Negative values indicate reverse counting from the top of the stack (i.e. -1 indicates the stack top)

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error, otherwise the string type

function.name: simGetStackSize
function.description: Returns the size of the stack

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: a stack handle obtained with simCreateStack

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error, otherwise the size of the stack.

function.name: simGetStackStringValue
function.description: Tries to retrieve the value at the top of the stack, if that value is a string

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: a stack handle obtained with simCreateStack

param.name: stringSize
param.description: a pointer to a location receiving the size of the string. Can be null if the string size is of no interest.

param.name: return
param.description:
in case of an error, the return value is null and
stringSize
is set to -1. If the stack item is not a string, the return value is null and
stringSize
is set to 0. If the stack item is a string, the return value is not null, and
stringSize
is the size of the string buffer (in that case, the user is in charge of releasing the returned buffer with simReleaseBuffer)

function.name: simGetStackMatrix
function.description: Tries to retrieve the value at the top of the stack, if that value is a matrix

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: a stack handle obtained with simCreateStack

param.name: rows
param.description: a pointer to a location receiving the number of rows. Can be null

param.name: cols
param.description: a pointer to a location receiving the number of columns. Can be null

param.name: return
param.description:
in case of an error, the return value is null and
rows
/
cols
are set to -1. If the stack item is not a matrix, the return value is null and
rows
/
cols
are set to 0. The user is in charge of releasing the returned buffer with simReleaseBuffer

function.name: simGetStackQuaternion
function.description: Tries to retrieve the value at the top of the stack, if that value is a quaternion

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: a stack handle obtained with simCreateStack

param.name: return
param.description: if the stack item is not a quaternion, the return value is null. The user is in charge of releasing the returned buffer with simReleaseBuffer

function.name: simGetStackPose
function.description: Tries to retrieve the value at the top of the stack, if that value is a pose

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: a stack handle obtained with simCreateStack

param.name: return
param.description: if the stack item is not a pose, the return value is null. The user is in charge of releasing the returned buffer with simReleaseBuffer

function.name: simGetStackColor4
function.description: Tries to retrieve the value at the top of the stack, if that value is a color

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: a stack handle obtained with simCreateStack

param.name: return
param.description: the RGBA color. If the stack item is not a color, the return value is null. The user is in charge of releasing the returned buffer with simReleaseBuffer

function.name: simGetStackTableInfo
function.description: Tries to retrieve information about a possible table at the top of the stack

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: stack handle obtained with simCreateStack

param.name: infoType
param.description:
type of information desired:
0 : whether we have a table, whether the table is an array-type or map-type, and the size of the array-type table
1 : whether the table contains only null values
2 : whether the table contains only number values
3 : whether the table contains only bool values
4 : whether the table contains only string values
5 : whether the table contains only table values
6 : whether the table contains only matrices
7 : whether the table contains only quaternions
8 : whether the table contains only poses
9 : whether the table contains only handles
10 : whether the table contains only colors

param.name: return
param.description:
-1 in case of an error, otherwise one of following:
if infoType is 0: a stack table information value . If the returned value is >0, then this means the table is an array-type table, containing value-key pairs, where all the keys are numbers, consecutive, and starting at 1. Use simUnfoldStackTable to read the content of the table
if infoType is 1: 1 if the table contains only null values
if infoType is 2: 1 if the table contains only number values
if infoType is 3: 1 if the table contains only bool values
if infoType is 4: 1 if the table contains only string values
if infoType is 5: 1 if the table contains only table values
if infoType is 6: 1 if the table contains only matrices

function.name: simGetStackUInt8Table
function.description: Retrieves a uint8 array from an array-type table at the top of the stack. Table values that are not numbers are converted to 0

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: a stack handle obtained with simCreateStack

param.name: array
param.description: a pointer to a location receiving the uint8 values. Use simGetStackTableInfo to determine the number of values the table contains.

param.name: count
param.description: the size of the array. If the array is bigger than the table, it will be padded with 0.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error, 0 if item is not an array or does not contain only numbers, 1 otherwise.

function.name: simInsertDataIntoStackTable
function.description:
Inserts data into a table on the stack. The function expects a
value
at the top of the stack, a
key
one position below, and a table below that. The value and its associated key will be inserted into the table and removed from the stack. If successive values are inserted with consecutive number keys starting at 1, then the table values can be accessed via number indices in a script, and the table can be seen as an array. Otherwise, the table can be seen as a map or associative array

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: a stack handle obtained with simCreateStack

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error.

function.name: simMoveStackItemToTop
function.description: Moves a stack into to the top of the stack

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: a stack handle obtained with simCreateStack

param.name: cIndex
param.description: the zero-based index of the item to move. Negative values indicate reverse counting from the top of the stack (i.e. -1 indicates the stack top)

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error.

function.name: simPushBoolOntoStack
function.description: Pushes a bool value onto the stack. The value will then be located at the top of the stack

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: stack handle obtained with simCreateStack

param.name: value
param.description: value to push

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error.

function.name: simAddForce
function.description: Adds a non-central force to a shape object that is dynamically enabled. Added forces are cumulative, applied relative to the center of mass, and are reset to zero after the physics engine advanced by one simulation time step (or by using the following flag: sim_handleflag_resetforcetorque)

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of a dynamically enabled shape. Can be combined with sim_handleflag_resetforcetorque in order to clear the accumulated force and torque.

param.name: position
param.description: pointer to 3 values that represent the relative position where the force should be applied.

param.name: force
param.description: pointer to 3 values that represent the force (in relative coordinates) to add.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simAddForceAndTorque
function.description: Adds a force and/or torque to a shape object that is dynamically enabled. Forces are applied at the center of mass. Added forces and torques are cumulative, and are reset to zero after the physics engine advanced by one simulation time step (or by using the following flags: sim_handleflag_resetforce and/or sim_handleflag_resettorque)

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of a dynamically enabled shape. Can be combined with sim_handleflag_resetforce and/or sim_handleflag_resettorque in order to clear the accumulated force or torque.

param.name: force
param.description: pointer to 3 values that represent the force (in absolute coordinates) to add. Can be null.

param.name: torque
param.description: pointer to 3 values that represent the torque (in absolute coordinates) to add. Can be null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simAddGraphCurve
function.description: Adds or updates a graph curve

param.name: graphHandle
param.description: handle of the graph

param.name: curveName
param.description: name of the curve

param.name: dim
param.description: dimension of the curve. Valid values are 2 or 3

param.name: streamIds
param.description: a pointer to 2 or 3 graph streams (x/y curves require 2 stream IDs, xyz curves require 3 stream IDs). Indicate -1 for a missing stream, in which case a default value will be used instead

param.name: defaultValues
param.description: a pointer to 2 or 3 default values

param.name: unitStr
param.description: string describing the unit of the curve. Can be null

param.name: options
param.description: bit-coded: bit0 is not used. If bit1 is set (2), the label is not visible. If bit2 is set (4), the points are not linked.

param.name: color
param.description: rgb-triplet, with values in [0.0-1.0], indicating the color of the stream. Can be null

param.name: curveWidth
param.description: the width of an xyz curve

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the id of the created/updated curve

function.name: simAddGraphStream
function.description: Adds or updates a graph stream

param.name: graphHandle
param.description: handle of the graph

param.name: streamName
param.description: name of the stream

param.name: unitStr
param.description: string describing the unit of the stream. Can be null

param.name: options
param.description: bit-coded: if bit0 is set (1), the stream is not visible. If bit1 is set (2), the label is not visible. If bit2 is set (4), the points are not linked.

param.name: color
param.description: rgb-triplet, with values in [0.0-1.0], indicating the color of the stream. Can be null

param.name: cyclicRange
param.description: can be used with cyclic values (e.g. angles) for correct data interpretation during stream data transformation . Set to 0 if not used

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the id of the created/updated stream

function.name: simAddItemToCollection
function.description: Adds an item to a collection

param.name: collectionHandle
param.description: the handle of a collection.

param.name: what
param.description:
the type of object (or group of objects) to add. Following are allowed values:
sim_handle_single
(for a single object),
sim_handle_all
(for all objects in the scene),
sim_handle_tree
(for a tree of objects), or
sim_handle_chain
(for a chain of objects (i.e. an inverted tree)).

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: the handle of an object.

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded options:
bit 0 set (1): the specified object (or group of objects) is removed from the collection. Otherwise it is added.
bit 1 set (2): the specified object is not included in the group of objects, if sim_handle_tree or sim_handle_chain is specified (i.e. the tree base or tip is excluded).

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful.

function.name: simAddLog
function.description: Adds a log message that will be output in the console or status bar

param.name: pluginName
param.description:
the name of the plugin that outputs the message. e.g.
simIK

param.name: verbosity
param.description: the verbosity level . Most appropriate values are sim_verbosity_errors (for errors), sim_verbosity_warnings (for warning) and sim_verbosity_infos.

param.name: message
param.description: the message. Set to null to clear the status bar

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful.

function.name: simAdjustView
function.description: Adjusts parameters of a view

param.name: viewHandleOrIndex
param.description: the handle of the view (can also be a floating view), or the index of the view.

param.name: associatedViewableObjectHandle
param.description: handle of the object that you wish to associate with the view. Must be a viewable object. Can also be -1, in which case the view is emptied

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0-bit5 = 0
bit6 (64) set=floating view is removed at simulation end
bit7 (128) set=floating view is ignored during a scene save operation
bit8 (256) set=the view is not modified. The return value of the function indicates if the view still exists (2), or does not exist anymore (1). No error is generated.
bit9 (512) set=the view is not modified. The return value of the function represents the object associated with the view.

param.name: return
param.description: a value >0 in case of success

function.name: simAlignShapeBB
function.description: Reorients the bounding box of a shape, while keeping the shape frame in place. The shape's inertia properties are unaffected

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape

param.name: pose
param.description: pose describing the new orientation of the shape's bounding box. The pose is expressed as [x y z qx qy qz qw], where the positional part is ignored. A null pose, or a pose containing only zeros will align the bounding box with the mesh's natural bounding box

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful. 0 if the bounding box could not be reoriented (the bounding box of primitive shapes cannot be reoriented), otherwise 1.

function.name: simAnnounceSceneContentChange
function.description: Announces a change in the scene. This is required for the undo/redo function to operate properly when performing changes via the API. Only call this function directly after a change was made through a dialog element (e.g. a checkbox was checked/unchecked) and that change was reported to the scene. What this call will do is following: the whole scene will be serialized (saved) to memory as a "scene image" and compared to a previously memorized "scene image". If both images are same, then the last image is discarded, otherwise only the changes between the two images are memorized. A call to this function has no effect (and doesn't generate any error) when called during simulation or when in edit mode.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, 0 if nothing was memorized, or 1 if changes were memorized.

function.name: simApplyTexture
function.description: Applies a texture via texture coordinates to a shape

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape

param.name: textureCoordinates
param.description: UV texture coordinates. For each of the shape's triangle, there should be exactly 3 UV texture coordinate pairs

param.name: textCoordSize
param.description: size of the textureCoordinate array

param.name: texture
param.description: RGB or RGBA texture

param.name: textureResolution
param.description: X/Y resolution of the texture

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0: if set (1), then adjacent texture pixels are not interpolated
bit1: if set (2), then the texture is applied as a decal (its appearance won't be influenced by light conditions)
bit2: unused, keep unset
bit3: unused, keep unset
bit4: if set (16), then the texture is RGBA, otherwise it is RGB
bit5: if set (32), then the texture is horizontally flipped
bit6: if set (64), then the texture is vertically flipped

param.name: return
param.description: The ID of the texture, or -1 in case of an error

function.name: simBuildMatrix
function.description: Builds a transformation matrix based on a position vector and Euler angles

param.name: position
param.description: pointer to 3 values

param.name: eulerAngles
param.description: pointer to 3 values

param.name: matrix
param.description: matrix (array of 12 values [Vx0 Vy0 Vz0 P0 Vx1 Vy1 Vz1 P1 Vx2 Vy2 Vz2 P2])

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simBuildPose
function.description: Builds a pose based on a position vector and Euler angles or axes

param.name: position
param.description: pointer to 3 values

param.name: eulerAngles
param.description: pointer to 3 values

param.name: pose
param.description: pose (array of 7 values [x y z qx qy qz qw])

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simCallScriptFunctionEx
function.description:
Calls a script function (from a plugin , the main client application , or from another script). This represents a user callback inside of a script. The target script must be initialized for this call to succeed, e.g. when calling simulation scripts , then simulation must be running
Data exchange between a plugin and a script happens via a stack . Reading and writing arguments from/to the stack gives you a maximum of flexibility, and you wil be able to exchange also complex data structures. But it can also be tedious and error prone. Use instead the helper classes located in
programming/include/simStack
: they will greatly simplify the task. Have a look at the example plugins simSkeleton and simSkel

param.name: scriptHandle
param.description: the handle of the script, or -1 for the sandbox script, or -2 for the main script

param.name: functionName
param.description: the function to call.

param.name: stackId
param.description: a stack handle . The stack represents the function's in/out values. See also the available stack functions .

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error

function.name: simCameraFitToView
function.description: Shifts and adjusts a camera associated with a view to fill the view entirely with the specified objects or models

param.name: viewHandleOrIndex
param.description:
the handle of the view (can also be a floating view), or the index of the view. If the camera is not associated with any view, then you can specify the handle of the camera, together with the
sim_handleflag_camera
flag.

param.name: objectCount
param.description: number of intems in the objectHandles pointer. Can be 0, in which case the whole visible scene will be filling the view.

param.name: objectHandles
param.description: pointer to objectHandles. Only visible objects will be taken into account. Can be null, in which case the whole visible scene will be filling the view.

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 (1): if set, then individual objects will be filling the view. If not set, then models associated with model base objects will also be included
bit1 (2): if set, then the view proportions will be 1 by 1, independently on what the view size is

param.name: scaling
param.description: a scaling factor

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful. 0 for a silent error (e.g. when the indicated view doesn't exist anymore), 1 for success

function.name: simCheckCollision
function.description: Checks whether two entities are colliding. The collidable flags of the entities are overridden if the entities are objects. If the entities are both the same collection (i.e. with the same collection handle), then same objects will not be checked against themselve

param.name: entity1Handle
param.description: handle of entity 1 (can be an object handle or a collection handle)

param.name: entity2Handle
param.description: handle of entity 2 (can be an object handle or a collection handle), or sim_handle_all to check entity1 against all other collidable objects

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error, 0 or 1 to indicate a collision state

function.name: simCheckDistance
function.description: Checks the minimum distance between two entities. The measurable flags of the entities are overridden if the entities are objects. If the entities are both the same collection (i.e. with the same collection handle), then same objects will not be checked against themselve

param.name: entity1Handle
param.description: handle of entity 1 (can be an object handle or a collection handle)

param.name: entity2Handle
param.description: handle of entity 2 (can be an object handle or a collection handle), or sim_handle_all to check entity1 against all other measurable objects

param.name: threshold
param.description: if distance is bigger than the threshold, the distance is not calculated and return value is 0. If threshold is 0 or negative, then no threshold is used.

param.name: distanceData
param.description: distanceData[0]-distanceData[5] represents the distance segment, distanceData[6] is the distance between the entities. This data is valid only if return value is 1

param.name: return
param.description: 0 or 1 if operation was successful (1 if distance is smaller than threshold), -1 otherwise

function.name: checkOctreePointOccupancy
function.description: Checks whether the provided points collide with the OC tree voxels

param.name: octreeHandle
param.description: handle of the OC tree

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): specified points are relative to the OC tree reference frame, otherwise they are relative to the world reference frame

param.name: pts
param.description: pointer to the points specified as x/y/z coordinates

param.name: ptCnt
param.description: number of points contained in pts

param.name: tag
param.description: pointer to a tag value, receiving the tag value of the voxel that collides with a single point. If several points are tested, then this pointer is ignored. Can be null.

param.name: location
param.description:
pointer to a uint64 value, which specifies the location of the voxel that collides with a single point. If several points are tested, then this pointer is ignored. Can be null. The location value is coded in following way:
bit0 - bit5: the depth level of the voxel in the OC tree structure (1-63).
bit6 - bit63: a triple bit-value for each depth level. triple bit-values represent the node location relative to the parent node:
0: (-1,-1,-1)
1: (+1,-1,-1)
2: (-1,+1,-1)
3: (+1,+1,-1)
4: (-1,-1,+1)
5: (+1,-1,+1)
6: (-1,+1,+1)
7: (+1,+1,+1)

param.name: reserved
param.description: set to null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, 0 if the points do not collide with the voxels, 1 if the points collide with the voxels

function.name: simCheckProximitySensor
function.description: Checks whether the proximity sensor detects the indicated entity. Detection is silent and the detectable flags of the entity are overridden if the entity is an object

param.name: sensorHandle
param.description: handle of the proximity sensor object

param.name: entityHandle
param.description: handle of entity to detect (object or collection), or sim_handle_all to detect all detectable objects

param.name: detectedPoint
param.description: coordinates of detected point relative to the sensor origin (detectedPoint[0]-detectedPoint[2]), and distance of detected point to the sensor origin (detectedPoint[3]). Can be null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise 0 (no detection) or 1 (detection)

function.name: simCheckVisionSensor
function.description: Checks whether the vision sensor detects the indicated entity. Detection is silent and the visibility state of the entity is overridden if the entity is an object

param.name: sensorHandle
param.description: handle of the vision sensor object

param.name: entityHandle
param.description: handle of entity to detect (object or collection), or sim_handle_all to detect all detectable objects

param.name: auxValues
param.description: by default CoppeliaSim returns one packet of 15 auxiliary values: the minimum of intensity, red, green, blue, depth value, the maximum of intensity, red, green, blue, depth value, and the average of intensity, red, green, blue, depth value. Additional packets can be appended in the vision callback functions . AuxValues can be null. The user is in charge of releasing the auxValues buffer with simReleaseBuffer (*auxValues).

param.name: auxValuesCount
param.description: contains information about the number of auxiliary value packets and packet sizes returned in auxValues. The first value is the number of packets, the second is the size of packet1, the third is the size of packet2, etc. Can be null if auxValues is also null. The user is in charge of releasing the auxValuesCount buffer with simReleaseBuffer (*auxValuesCount)

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise 0 (no detection) or 1 (detection)

function.name: simCheckVisionSensorEx
function.description: Checks whether the vision sensor detects the indicated entity. Detection is silent and the visibility state of the entity is overridden if the entity is an object

param.name: sensorHandle
param.description: handle of the vision sensor object

param.name: entityHandle
param.description: handle of entity to detect (object or collection), or sim_handle_all to detect all detectable objects

param.name: returnImage
param.description: specifies what should be returned. If true, the sensor's image buffer is returned, otherwise its depth buffer is returned

param.name: return
param.description: image or depth buffer, or null in case of an error. The user is in charge of releasing the returned buffer with simReleaseBuffer

function.name: simCloseScene
function.description: Closes current scene, and switches to another open scene. If there is no other open scene, a new scene is then created. Can only be called from an add-on , or from the sanbox script, when called from within CoppeliaSim

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the current scene index.

function.name: simComputeMassAndInertia
function.description: Computes and applies the mass and inertia matrix for a shape, based on its convex representation.

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of shape

param.name: density)
param.description: the density expressed in kg/m^3

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error, 0 if the shape is not convex, otherwise 1.

function.name: simConvexDecompose
function.description: Calculates the convex decomposition of a shape using the HACD or V-HACD algorithms

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape to operate on

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): the specified shape will be morphed into its convex decomposition. Otherwise, the convex decomposition will smply be added to the scene
bit1 set (2): specified convex decomposition parameters will be displayed in a dialog, allowing the user to modify them.
bit2 set (4): same convex decomposition parameters will be used as a previous call to this function. Only when this bit is set can the convex decomposition parameters be omitted.
bit3 set (8): HACD: extra points will be added when computing the concavity
bit4 set (16): HACD: faces points will be added when computing the concavity
bit5 (32): reserved. Do not set
bit6 (64): reserved. Do not set
bit7 set (128): the V-HACD algorithm will be used. If not set, the HACD algorithm will be used.
bit8 set (256): V-HACD: pca is enabled (default is disabled).
bit9 set (512): V-HACD: tetrahedron-based approximate convex decomposition. If not set, then the voxel-based approximate convex decomposition will be used (default).

param.name: intParams
param.description:
10 int values:
intParams[0]: HACD: the minimum number of clusters to be generated (e.g. 1)
intParams[1]: HACD: the targeted number of triangles of the decimated mesh (e.g. 500)
intParams[2]: HACD: the maximum number of vertices for each generated convex hull (e.g. 100)
intParams[3]: reserved. Set to 0
intParams[4]: reserved. Set to 0
intParams[5]: V-HACD: resolution (10000-64000000, 100000 is default).
intParams[6]: V-HACD: depth (1-32, 20 is default).
intParams[7]: V-HACD: plane downsampling (1-16, 4 is default).
intParams[8]: V-HACD: convex hull downsampling (1-16, 4 is default).
intParams[9]: V-HACD: max. number of vertices per convex hull (4-1024, 64 is default).

param.name: floatParams
param.description:
10 double values:
floatParams[0]: HACD: the maximum allowed concavity (e.g. 100.0)
floatParams[1]: HACD: the maximum allowed distance to get convex clusters connected (e.g. 30)
floatParams[2]: HACD: the threshold to detect small clusters. The threshold is expressed as a percentage of the total mesh surface (e.g. 0.25)
floatParams[3]: reserved. Set to 0.0
floatParams[4]: reserved. Set to 0.0
floatParams[5]: V-HACD: concavity (0.0-1.0, 0.0025 is default).
floatParams[6]: V-HACD: alpha (0.0-1.0, 0.05 is default).
floatParams[7]: V-HACD: beta (0.0-1.0, 0.05 is default).
floatParams[8]: V-HACD: gamma (0.0-1.0, 0.00125 is default).
floatParams[9]: V-HACD: min. volume per convex hull (0.0-0.01, 0.0001 is default).

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful. Otherwise the handle of the new shape, or the handle of the original shape when morphing.

function.name: simCopyPasteObjects
function.description: Copies and pastes objects

param.name: objectHandles
param.description: array containing the handles of the objects to copy and paste. The same array will receive the copied object handles, with the same correspondency.

param.name: objectCount
param.description: the number of handles contained in the objectHandles array.

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded. If bit0 is set (i.e. 1), then whole models will be copied. In that case, all specified objects should be flagged as
model base
. If bit1 is set (i.e. 2), then all simulation and customization scripts are stripped away. If bit2 is set (i.e. 4), then all object custom data properties are stripped away. If bit3 is set (i.e. 8), then all object references are stripped away. If bit4 is set (i.e. 16), then all textures are stripped away. If bit5 is set (i.e. 32), then model tags and DNA information is stripped away.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful.

function.name: simCreateCollectionEx
function.description: Creates a new collection . A collection created in a simulation script , a customization script or in the main script are automatically destroyed when the script ends

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded options:
bit 0 set (1): collection overrides collidable, measurable, detectable properties, and also the visibility state of its objects.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the handle of the new collection.

function.name: simCreateDummy
function.description: Creates a dummy .

param.name: size
param.description: the dummy size

param.name: reserved
param.description: set to null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the handle of the dummy

function.name: simCreateScript
function.description: Creates a script object .

param.name: scriptType
param.description: the script type . Only simulation-, customization- or passive scripts are allowed

param.name: scriptText
param.description: the content of the script

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded options:
bit 0 set (1): script is initially disabled

param.name: lang
param.description: language. A value different from "lua" or "python" disables the script. null defaults to "lua"

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the handle of the script object

function.name: simGetObjectHierarchyOrder
function.description: Retrieves the zero-based position of an object among its siblings in the scene hierarchy

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: the handle of the object

param.name: totalSiblingsCount
param.description: if not null, receives the total siblings count

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the position of the object

function.name: simSetObjectHierarchyOrder
function.description: Moves an object up or down among its siblings in the scene hierarchy

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: the handle of the object

param.name: order
param.description: the desired zero-based position

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simCreateForceSensor
function.description: Creates a force sensor .

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded options:
bit 0 set (1): force threshold enabled
bit 1 set (2): torque threshold enabled

param.name: intParams
param.description:
5 integer parameters:
intParams[0]: filter type (0=average, 1=median)
intParams[1]: value count the filter operates on
intParams[2]: number of consecutive threshold violation for the sensor to break
intParams[3]: reserved. Set to 0
intParams[4]: reserved. Set to 0

param.name: floatParams
param.description:
5 floating point parameters:
floatParams[0]: sensor size
floatParams[1]: force threshold value
floatParams[2]: torque threshold value
floatParams[3]: reserved. Set to 0.0
floatParams[4]: reserved. Set to 0.0

param.name: reserved
param.description: set to null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the handle of the force sensor

function.name: simCreateHeightfieldShape
function.description: Creates a heightfield shape

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded options:
bit0 set (1): back faces are culled
bit1 set (2): overlay mesh is visible
bit2 set (4): a simple shape is generated instead of a heightfield
bit3 set (8): the heightfield is not respondable

param.name: shadingAngle
param.description: the shading angle

param.name: xPointCount
param.description: the number of rows of the heightfield

param.name: yPointCount
param.description: the number of lines of the heightfield

param.name: xSize
param.description: the length of the x side of the heightfield

param.name: heights
param.description: a pointer to xPointCount*yPointCount height values

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the handle of the newly created shape

function.name: simCreateJoint
function.description: Creates a joint

param.name: jointMode
param.description: a joint mode

param.name: options
param.description: reserved. Set to 0

param.name: sizes
param.description: pointer to 2 values indicating the joint length and diameter. Can be null for default values

param.name: reservedA
param.description: set to null

param.name: reservedB
param.description: set to null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the handle of the joint

function.name: simCreateOctree
function.description: Creates an empty OC tree

param.name: voxelSize
param.description: size of the voxels

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): voxels have random colors
bit1 set (2): show OC tree structure
bit2 set (4): show points instead of voxels
bit3 set (8): reserved. keep unset
bit4 set (16): color is emissive

param.name: pointSize
param.description: the size of the points in pixels, when voxels are rendered with points

param.name: reserved
param.description: set to null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the handle of the OC tree

function.name: simCreatePointCloud
function.description: Creates an empty point cloud

param.name: maxVoxelSize
param.description: maximum size of the OC tree voxels containing points

param.name: maxPtCntPerVoxel
param.description: maximum number of points allowed in a same OC tree voxel

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): points have random colors
bit1 set (2): show OC tree structure
bit2 set (4): reserved. keep unset
bit3 set (8): do not use an OC tree structure. When enabled, point cloud operations are limited, and point clouds will not be collidable , measurable or detectable anymore, but adding points will be much faster
bit4 set (16): color is emissive

param.name: pointSize
param.description: size of the points, in pixels

param.name: reserved
param.description: set to null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the handle of the point cloud

function.name: simCreatePrimitiveShape
function.description: Creates a primitive shape

param.name: primitiveType
param.description: the primitive type of the shape

param.name: sizes
param.description: 3 values indicating the size of the shape

param.name: options
param.description:
Bit-coded:
bit0 is set (1): backfaces are culled
bit1 is set (2): edges appear sharp
bit2 is set (4): cylinder has open ends
bit3 is set (8): the primitive shape is not registered as such by CoppeliaSim, but rather as a random (maybe convex) shape

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the handle of the newly created shape

function.name: simCreateProximitySensor
function.description: Creates a proximity sensor

param.name: sensorType
param.description: desired proximity sensor type (e.g. sim_proximitysensor_cone)

param.name: subType
param.description: deprecated. Set to 16

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded options:
bit0 set (1): the sensor will be explicitly handled
bit1 set (2): reserved. Set to 0
bit2 set (4): the detection volume is not shown
bit3 set (8): front faces are not detected
bit4 set (16): back faces are not detected
bit5 set (32): fast detection (i.e. not exact detection)
bit6 set (64): the normal of the detected surface with the detection ray will have to lie below a specified threshold angle
bit8 set (256): smallest distance threshold will be active
bit9 set (512): randomized detection (only with ray-type proximity sensors)

param.name: intParams
param.description:
8 integer parameters:
intParams[0]: face count (volume description)
intParams[1]: face count far (volume description)
intParams[2]: subdivisions (volume description)
intParams[3]: subdivisions far (volume description)
intParams[4]: reserved. Set to 1
intParams[5]: reserved. Set to 1
intParams[6]: reserved. Set to 0
intParams[7]: reserved. Set to 0

param.name: floatParams
param.description:
15 floating point parameters:
floatParams[0]: offset (volume description)
floatParams[1]: range (volume description)
floatParams[2]: x size (volume description)
floatParams[3]: y size (volume description)
floatParams[4]: x size far (volume description)
floatParams[5]: y size far (volume description)
floatParams[6]: inside gap (volume description)
floatParams[7]: radius (volume description)
floatParams[8]: radius far (volume description)
floatParams[9]: angle (volume description)
floatParams[10]: threshold angle for limited angle detection (see bit 6 above)
floatParams[11]: smallest detection distance (see bit 8 above)
floatParams[12]: sensing point size
floatParams[13]: reserved. Set to 0.0
floatParams[14]: reserved. Set to 0.0

param.name: reserved
param.description: set to null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the handle of the force sensor

function.name: simCreateShape
function.description: Creates a mesh shape

param.name: options
param.description:
Bit-coded:
if bit0 is set (1), backfaces are culled
If bit1 is set (2), edges are visible
If bit2 is set (4), adjacent texture pixels are not interpolated
If bit3 is set (8), texture is applied as a decal
If bit4 is set (16), texture is RGBA, otherwise it is RGB
If bit5 is set (32), texture is horizontally flipped
If bit6 is set (64), texture is vertically flipped

param.name: shadingAngle
param.description: shading angle

param.name: vertices
param.description: array of vertices

param.name: verticesSize
param.description: size of the vertice array

param.name: indices
param.description: array of indices

param.name: indicesSize
param.description: size of the indice array

param.name: normals
param.description: optional array of normal vectors. Has to be 3*indicesSize in length

param.name: textureCoordinates
param.description: optional array of texture coordinates. Has to be 2*indicesSize in length

param.name: texture
param.description: optional texture, specified as RGB or RGBA

param.name: textureResolution
param.description: resolution of the specified texture

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the handle of the newly created shape

function.name: simCreateTexture
function.description: Creates a planar shape, that will be textured with a new, or imported texture

param.name: fileName
param.description: filename of the texure to import, or an empty string if you wish to create a new texture.

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): do not interpolate adjacent color patches.
bit1 set (2): apply the texture in decal-mode.
bit2 set (4): repeat the texture along the U direction.
bit3 set (8): repeat the texture along the V direction.

param.name: planeSizes
param.description: a pointer to 2 values: the dimensions of the planar shape that will be generated. Can be null for default dimensions

param.name: scalingUV
param.description: a pointer to 2 values: the desired scaling of the texture, along the U and V directions. Can be null for default scalings

param.name: xy_g
param.description: a pointer to 3 values: the texture x/y shift, and the texture gamma-rotation. Can be null for default shift/rotation values

param.name: fixedResolution
param.description: 0 to import the shape with its original resolution. Otherwise, specify a power of 2 value which will be the maximum texture resolution (the texture will also be applied a power of 2 resolution)

param.name: textureId
param.description: a pointer to an integer that will be used to return the new texture ID. If a same texture is already present, the old texture ID will be returned. Can be null

param.name: resolution
param.description: a pointer to 2 values representing the desired texture resolution when creating a new texture. The same pointer is used to return the effective resolution of the created/imported texture

param.name: reserved
param.description: set to null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error, otherwise the object handle of the created planar shape.

function.name: simCreateVisionSensor
function.description: Creates a vision sensor .

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded options:
bit 0 set (1): the sensor will be explicitly handled
bit 1 set (2): the sensor will be in perspective operation mode
bit 2 set (4): the view frustum will not be shown
bit 3 set (8): reserved. Set to 0
bit 4 set (16): the sensor will be passive (use an external image)
bit 5 set (32): the sensor will use local lights
bit 6 set (64): the sensor will not render any fog
bit 7 set (128): the sensor will use a specific color for default background (i.e. null pixels)

param.name: intParams
param.description:
4 integer parameters:
intParams[0]: sensor resolution x
intParams[1]: sensor resolution y
intParams[2]: reserved. Set to 0
intParams[3]: reserved. Set to 0

param.name: floatParams
param.description:
11 floating point parameters:
floatParams[0]: near clipping plane
floatParams[1]: far clipping plane
floatParams[2]: view angle / ortho view size
floatParams[3]: sensor size x
floatParams[4]: reserved. Set to 0.0
floatParams[5]: reserved. Set to 0.0
floatParams[6]: null pixel red-value
floatParams[7]: null pixel green-value
floatParams[8]: null pixel blue-value
floatParams[9]: reserved. Set to 0.0
floatParams[10]: reserved. Set to 0.0

param.name: reserved
param.description: set to null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the handle of the force sensor

function.name: simDestroyCollection
function.description: Removes a collection

param.name: collectionHandle
param.description: handle of the collection to remove

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simDestroyGraphCurve
function.description: Destroys a graph stream or curve

param.name: graphHandle
param.description: handle of the graph

param.name: curveId
param.description: the id of the stream or curve, -1 to remove all streams/curves

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simDoesFileExist
function.description: Indicates whether a file exists.

param.name: filename
param.description: The filename extension is required

param.name: return
param.description: 1 if the filename exists, 0 if it does not exist, or -1 in case of an error

function.name: simDuplicateGraphCurveToStatic
function.description: Duplicates a graph stream or curve, and freezes it

param.name: graphHandle
param.description: handle of the graph

param.name: curveId
param.description: the id of the stream or curve

param.name: staticCurveName
param.description: name of the new static stream/curve

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the id of the created static stream or curve

function.name: simExecuteScriptString
function.description:
Executes some code in a specific script (from a plugin , the main client application , or from another script). The target script must be initialized for this call to succeed, e.g. when calling simulation scripts , then simulation must be running
From C/C++, data exchange between a plugin and a script happens via a stack . Reading and writing arguments from/to the stack gives you a maximum of flexibility, and you wil be able to exchange also complex data structures. But it can also be tedious and error prone. Use instead the helper classes located in
programming/include/simStack

param.name: scriptHandle
param.description: the handle of the script

param.name: stringToExecute
param.description: a string representing the code to execute in the specified script

param.name: stackId
param.description: 0 (for no stack) or a stack handle . The stack holds possible out values. See also the available stack functions .

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error

function.name: simExportMesh
function.description: Exports a mesh to a file

param.name: fileformat
param.description:
the fileformat to export to:
0: OBJ format
3: TEXT STL format
4: BINARY STL format
5: COLLADA format
6: TEXT PLY format
7: BINARY PLY format

param.name: pathAndFilename
param.description: the location of the file to create

param.name: options
param.description: keep at 0

param.name: scalingFactor
param.description: the scaling factor to apply to the vertices to export

param.name: elementCount
param.description: the number of meshes

param.name: vertices
param.description: an array to vertice arrays

param.name: verticesSizes
param.description: an array indicating the individual vertice array sizes

param.name: indices
param.description: an array to indice arrays

param.name: indicesSizes
param.description: an array indicating the individual indice array sizes

param.name: reserved
param.description: set to null

param.name: names
param.description: set to null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simFloatingViewAdd
function.description: Adds a floating view to current page

param.name: posX
param.description: relative X position of the center of the floating view. Accepted values are between 0 and 1.

param.name: posY
param.description: relative Y position of the center of the floating view. Accepted values are between 0 and 1.

param.name: sizeX
param.description: relative X size of the floating view. Accepted values are between 0 and 1.

param.name: sizeY
param.description: relative Y size of the floating view. Accepted values are between 0 and 1.

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1)=double click allows swapping the floating view with the main view
bit1 set (2)=the floating view doesn't have a close button
bit2 set (4)=the floating view cannot be shifted
bit3 set (8)=the floating view cannot be resized

param.name: return
param.description: Handle of the floating view, or -1 in case of an error.

function.name: simFloatingViewRemove
function.description: Removes a floating view previously added with sim_floatingViewAdd .

param.name: floatingViewHandle
param.description: handle of the floating view to be removed

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error, 0 if the floating view could not be found (e.g. because closed by the user), or 1 if the floating view was closed.

function.name: simGenerateShapeFromPath
function.description: Generates an extrusion shape from a path

param.name: path
param.description: path, specified in row-major order, with [x y z qx qy qz qw] values for each path point. The orientation parts for each path point are ignored if the two first bits of are zero.

param.name: pathSize
param.description: size/length of the path pointer

param.name: section
param.description: extrusion profile, with x/y pairs, e.g. [x1 y1 x2 y2 ... xn yn]. If the first and last pair overlap, the section is closed

param.name: sectionSize
param.description: size/length of the section pointer

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0-bit1: 0 = upVector is upVector , 1 = upVector is the x-axis of the path point frame, 2 = upVector is the y-axis of the path point frame, 3 = upVector is the z-axis of the path point frame
bit2 set (4): the path is closed

param.name: upVector
param.description: vector that identifies the up-side of the extrusion shape. Can be null if the two first bits of options are non-zero

param.name: reserved
param.description: set to 0.0

param.name: return
param.description: the handle of the generated shape

function.name: simGetApiFunc
function.description: Retrieves all API functions and variables that match a specific word. Useful for script code auto-completion functionality

param.name: scriptHandle
param.description: handle of the script . Can be -1 to be script agnostic.

param.name: apiWord
param.description: word that API functions and variables should match, e.g. "sim_getObj". Only matches up to the first dot are returned, if the apiWord does not contain any dot. To retrieve all functions and variables, leave apiWord empty. To retrieve only functions, add '+' as prefix. To retrieve only variables, add '-' as prefix.

param.name: return
param.description: null in case of an error, or if there is no match. Otherwise all matching API functions and variables, space-separated. The user is in charge of releasing the returned buffer with simReleaseBuffer .

function.name: simGetApiInfo
function.description: Returns the call tip (or info text) for an API function

param.name: scriptHandle
param.description: handle of the script . Can be -1 to be script agnostic

param.name: apiWord
param.description: API functions or variable to retrieve the info for, e.g. "sim_getObject". Return is for the latest version of a module by default. For a specific module version, specify its full name, e.g. "sim-1.getObject". Using the wildcard, e.g. "sim-*.getObject", the returned information is prepended by its related module name (e.g. "[sim-1]"), for all available modules

param.name: return
param.description: null in case of an error. Otherwise the information related to the API function or variable. The user is in charge of releasing the returned buffer with simReleaseBuffer .

function.name: simGetClosestPosOnPath
function.description: Returns the position or distance along a path that is closest to a specified point in space

param.name: path
param.description: path, specified in row-major order, with x/y/z values for each path point

param.name: pathSize
param.description: size/length of the path pointer

param.name: pathLengths
param.description: lengths of a path. Each path point should have a corresponding length value (as the distance from the path's first point, along the path). See also sim_getPathLengths .

param.name: absPt
param.description: point in 3D space

param.name: return
param.description: position/distance along the path

function.name: simGetCollectionObjects
function.description: Retrieves the object handles that compose a collection

param.name: collectionHandle
param.description: handle of the collection

param.name: objectCount
param.description: pointer to a value receiving the number of returned object handles

param.name: return
param.description: pointer to n object handles, or null if an error occurred. The user is in charge of destroying the returned buffer with simReleaseBuffer

function.name: simGetContactInfo
function.description: Retrieves contact point information of a dynamic simulation pass.

param.name: dynamicPass
param.description: specific dynamic sub-step index or sim_handle_all. By default a call to simHandleDynamics executes the dynamics engine x times, with x times smaller time steps (where x is a parameter that can be adjusted). At each of those sub-steps, contacts are created and destroyed. With the dynamicPass argument you can select which sub-step you wish to retrieve contacts from (zero-based index), or sim_handle_all to retrieve the contacts of all sub-steps. See also the dynamicsStepSize property.

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of a specific object you wish to retrieve contacts from, or sim_handle_all to retrieve all contacts in the scene.

param.name: index
param.description: zero-based index of the contact to retrieve. Optionally, you may add sim_handleflag_extended to the index, if you also wish to retrieve the normal vector (see further down)

param.name: objectHandles
param.description: handles of the two objects contacting. The handles might also refer to particle objects that are not treated as regular scene objects.

param.name: contactInfo
param.description: pointer to 6 values (or 9 values if sim_handleflag_extended was added to index), where the 3 first values represent the contact position, the 3 next values represent the force generated by the contact, and the (optional) 3 last values represent the normal vector at the contact. The force vector returned by the Bullet engine will omit the component that results from fricition.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error, 0 if no contact was found at the given index or 1 if a contact was returned.

function.name: simGetDecimatedMesh
function.description: Retrieves a decimated mesh (i.e. a simplified mesh)

param.name: inVertices
param.description: pointer to the input vertices (succession of x/y/z values).

param.name: inVerticesL
param.description: number of input vertices times 3.

param.name: inIndices
param.description: pointer to the input indices (3 values for each triangle).

param.name: inIndicesL
param.description: number of input triangles times 3.

param.name: verticesOut
param.description: pointer to a pointer to the output vertices. The output vertices are allocated by CoppeliaSim and the user is in charge of releasing the buffer via simReleaseBuffer .

param.name: verticesOutL
param.description: pointer to the number of output vertices times 3.

param.name: indicesOut
param.description: pointer to a pointer to the output indices. The output indices are allocated by CoppeliaSim and the user is in charge of releasing the buffer via simReleaseBuffer .

param.name: indicesOutL
param.description: pointer to the number of output indices (i.e. the number of triangles times 3).

param.name: decimationPercent
param.description: percentage of the desired decimation (0.1-0.9).

param.name: reserved1
param.description: reserved, set to 0.

param.name: reserved2
param.description: reserved, set to null.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 or 0 if operation was not successful.

function.name: simGetEulerAnglesFromMatrix
function.description: Retrieves the Euler angles from a transformation matrix

param.name: matrix
param.description: array of 12 values [Vx0 Vy0 Vz0 P0 Vx1 Vy1 Vz1 P1 Vx2 Vy2 Vz2 P2]

param.name: eulerAngles
param.description: pointer to 3 values

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetExplicitHandling
function.description: Retrieves the explicit handling flags for a scene object

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of a scene object.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if command was not successful, otherwise the explicit handling flags for the specified object (for now only bit 0 is used).

function.name: simGetExtensionString
function.description: Retrieves a string that describes additional environment or object properties, mainly used by extension plugins.

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: the handle of an object, or -1 if you wish to retrieve the extension string of the environment.

param.name: index
param.description: keep to -1, unless the object is a shape, and you wish to retrieve the extension string of a shape component (since a shape can be a compound of several other shapes).

param.name: key
param.description:
an optional key indicating what value to retrieve. If none is specified, then the whole extension string will be returned. Keys should have the form of
key@parentKey@...@parentKey
. To retrieve the
shadow enabled
value of extension string "povray{ shadow {true} fadeXDist {0.00}}", specify following key:
shadow@povray
. The key is case sensitive.

param.name: return
param.description: a string if the operation was successful. The user is in charge of releasing the buffer with simReleaseBuffer .

function.name: simGetJointDependency
function.description: Retrieves joint dependency information, when the joint is in dependent mode

param.name: jointHandle
param.description: handle of the slave joint

param.name: masterJointHandle
param.description: handle of the master joint, or -1 if the joint is not dependent

param.name: offset
param.description: offset in equation slave = offset + master * multCoeff

param.name: multCoeff
param.description: coeff in equation slave = offset + master * multCoeff

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetJointForce
function.description: Retrieves the force or torque applied to a joint along/about its active axis. This function retrieves meaningful information only if the joint is prismatic or revolute, and is dynamically enabled. With the Bullet, MuJoCo and Newton engine, this function returns the force or torque applied to the joint motor (torques from joint limits are not taken into account). With the ODE and Vortex engine, this function returns the total force or torque applied to a joint along/about its z-axis

param.name: jointHandle
param.description: handle of the joint. Can be combined with sim_handleflag_rawvalue (simply add sim_handleflag_rawvalue to jointHandle), if you wish to access the raw values generated by each individual dynamic simulation step (by default, there are 10 dynamic simulation steps for each simulation step). Raw values can only be accessed from inside a callback function triggered by the physics engine.

param.name: forceOrTorque
param.description: the force or the torque applied to the joint along/about its z-axis.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful. 0 if no value is available yet (e.g. when simHandleDynamics hasn't yet handled that joint), otherwise a value >0.

function.name: simGetJointInterval
function.description: Retrieves the interval parameters of a joint

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the joint

param.name: cyclic
param.description: indicates whether the joint is cyclic (the joint varies between -pi and +pi in a cyclic manner)

param.name: interval
param.description: interval of the joint. interval[0] is the joint minimum allowed value, interval[1] is the joint range (the maximum allowed value is interval[0]+interval[1]). When the joint is "cyclic", then the interval parameters don't have any meaning.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetJointMode
function.description: Retrieves the operation mode of a joint

param.name: jointHandle
param.description: handle of the joint object

param.name: options
param.description: (output): not used

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the joint mode .

function.name: simGetJointPosition
function.description: Retrieves the linear/angular displacement/angle of a joint. This function cannot be used with spherical joints (use sim_getObjectChildPose instead)

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the joint

param.name: pos
param.description: linear/angular displacement/angle of the joint

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetJointTargetForce
function.description: Retrieves the force or torque that a joint can exert

param.name: jointHandle
param.description: handle of the joint.

param.name: forceOrTorque
param.description: the maximum force or torque the joint can apply. The sign indicates the desired movement direction.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful. .

function.name: simGetJointTargetPosition
function.description: Retrieves the target linear/angular displacement/angle of a joint

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the joint object

param.name: targetPosition
param.description: (output): target position of the joint

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetJointTargetVelocity
function.description: Retrieves the target linear/angular velocity of a non-spherical joint

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the joint object

param.name: targetVelocity
param.description: (output): target linear/angular velocity of the joint

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetJointType
function.description: Retrieves the type of a joint

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the joint

param.name: return
param.description: Type of the joint (sim_joint_revolute, sim_joint_prismatic or sim_joint_spherical), or -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetJointVelocity
function.description: Retrieves the linear or angular velocity of a joint. The velocity is a measured velocity (i.e. from one simulation step to the next), and is available for all joints in the scene

param.name: jointHandle

param.name: velocity
param.description: a pointer that will receive the velocity.

function.name: simGetLastInfo
function.description: Retrieves and clears the information string generated by last API call

param.name: return
param.description: the information string, if it exists. The user has to delete the returned buffer with a call to simReleaseBuffer

function.name: simGetLinkDummy
function.description: Retrieves the object handle of the dummy linked to this one

param.name: dummyHandle
param.description: handle of the dummy whose linked dummy has to be retrieved.

param.name: return
param.description: Handle of the dummy linked to the specified dummy object, or -1 if the dummy is not linked or in case of an error

function.name: simGetMainWindow
function.description: Retrieves the handle or pointer of the main window.

param.name: type
param.description: type of the desired return value. 0 for a native window handle, 1 for a pointer to a QWidget object.

param.name: return
param.description: a native window handle or a pointer to a QWidget object.

function.name: simGetNavigationMode
function.description: Retrieves the navigation and selection mode for the mouse

param.name: return
param.description: navigation mode if operation was successful, -1 otherwise

function.name: simGetObject
function.description: Retrieves an object handle based on its path and alias

param.name: objectPath
param.description: path and alias of the object to find. See the section on accessing scene objects for details.

param.name: index
param.description: fetches the n-th object (zero based index) that satisfies the object path. -1 returns the first object that satisfies the object path.

param.name: proxy
param.description: if different from -1, specifies an object handle that identifies a hierarchy tree to search in.

param.name: options
param.description: bit-coded. bit0 set (i.e. 1): errors are silent.

param.name: return
param.description: handle of object or -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetObjectAlias
function.description: Retrieves the alias or path of an object based on its handle

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object.

param.name: options
param.description:
alias formating options:
-1: naked alias (e.g. "alias")
0: ordered alias (e.g. "alias[0]")
1: short path that fully identifies the object (e.g. "/parent/alias[0]")
2: full path (e.g. "/parentA/parentB/alias[0]")
3: naked alias with handle, if the alias is not unique (e.g. "alias" or "alias__42__")
4: naked alias with handle (e.g. "alias__42__")
5: print version of the short path, not guaranteed to be unique (e.g. "/parent/ ... /alias")
6: path using just the alias and an index number (e.g. "/alias{3}")
7: same as 6, but using the first parent model, if present (e.g. "/parentModel{2}/alias{3}")
8: same as 6, but using the first 2 parent models, if present (e.g. "/parentModelA{4}/parentModelB{2}/alias{3}")
9: same as 6, but using all parent models, if present (e.g. "/parentModelA{4}/.../parentModelN{2}/alias{3}")

param.name: return
param.description: Alias or path of the object if operation was successful, null otherwise. The user is in charge of destroying the returned buffer with simReleaseBuffer

function.name: simGetObjectChild
function.description: Retrieves the handle of an object's child object

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object, or sim_handle_scene to retrieve orphan objects

param.name: index
param.description: zero-based index of the child's position. To retrieve all children of an object, call the function by increasing the index until the return value is -1

param.name: return
param.description: handle of child object or -1 if the child doesn't exist at that index or in case of an error

function.name: simGetObjectChildPose
function.description: Retrieves the intrinsic or internal transformation of an object. For a joint, this is the transformation caused by the joint movement, mainly. For joints and force sensors, this will also include a possible error transformation caused by the physics engine (a physics engine can cause joints and force sensors to come apart, when constraints can't be perfectly resolved)

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object

param.name: pose
param.description: pointer to the pose

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetObjectColor
function.description: Retrieves the color of a scene object

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object

param.name: index
param.description: the zero-based index of the color, if the object has several colors

param.name: colorComponent
param.description: a color component .

param.name: rgbData
param.description: (output): red, green and blue components of the color (3 values)

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful. 0 if the color index was not found in the object. 1 otherwise.

function.name: simGetObjectfromUid
function.description: Retrieves an object handle based on its unique identifier

param.name: objectUid
param.description: the object unique identifier.

param.name: options
param.description: bit-coded. bit0 set (i.e. 1): errors are silent.

param.name: return
param.description: handle of object or -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetObjectMatrix
function.description: Retrieves the transformation matrix of an object

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object. Can be combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe (see next argument)

param.name: relativeToObjectHandle
param.description: indicates relative to which reference frame we want the matrix. Specify sim_handle_world to retrieve the absolute transformation matrix, sim_handle_inverse to retrieve the inverse of the absolute transformation matrix, sim_handle_parent to retrieve the transformation matrix relative to the object's parent, or an object handle relative to whose reference frame we want the transformation matrix. If this handle is the handle of a joint, then the matrix relative to the joint's moving frame will be returned (unless is combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe, in which case the matrix relative to the joint's base frame will be returned).

param.name: matrix
param.description: pointer to 12 double values

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetObjectOrientation

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object. Can be combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe (see next argument)

param.name: relativeToObjectHandle
param.description: indicates relative to which reference frame we want the orientation. Specify sim_handle_world to retrieve the absolute orientation, sim_handle_inverse to retrieve the inverse of the absolute orientation, sim_handle_parent to retrieve the orientation relative to the object's parent, or an object handle relative to whose reference frame you want the orientation. If this handle is the handle of a joint, then the orientation relative to the joint's moving frame will be returned (unless is combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe, in which case the orientation relative to the joint's base frame will be returned).

param.name: eulerAngles
param.description: Euler angles (alpha, beta and gamma)

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetObjectParent
function.description: Retrieves the handle of an object's parent object

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object

param.name: return
param.description: handle of parent or -1 if the parent doesn't exist or in case of an error

function.name: simGetObjectPose
function.description: Retrieves the pose of an object

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object. Combine with sim_handleflag_wxyzquat to retrieve the quaternion as [qw qx qy qz] order instead of [qx qy qz qw] order. Can also be combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe (see next argument)

param.name: relativeToObjectHandle
param.description: indicates relative to which reference frame we want the pose. Specify sim_handle_world to retrieve the absolute pose, sim_handle_inverse to retrieve the inverse of the absolute pose, sim_handle_parent to retrieve the pose relative to the object's parent, or an object handle relative to whose reference frame we want the pose. If this handle is the handle of a joint, then the pose relative to the joint's moving frame will be returned (unless is combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe, in which case the pose relative to the joint's base frame will be returned).

param.name: pose
param.description: pointer to 7 values: [x y z qx qy qz qw]

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetObjectPosition
function.description: Retrieves the position of an object

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object. Can be combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe (see next argument)

param.name: relativeToObjectHandle
param.description: indicates relative to which reference frame we want the position. Specify sim_handle_world to retrieve the absolute position, sim_handle_parent to retrieve the position relative to the object's parent, or an object handle relative to whose reference frame we want the position. If this handle is the handle of a joint, then the position relative to the joint's moving frame will be returned (unless is combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe, in which case the position relative to the joint's base frame will be returned).

param.name: position
param.description: pointer to 3 values [x y z]

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetObjectQuaternion
function.description: Retrieves the quaternion of an object

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object. Combine with sim_handleflag_wxyzquat to retrieve the quaternion as [qw qx qy qz] order instead of [qx qy qz qw] order. Can also be combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe (see next argument)

param.name: relativeToObjectHandle
param.description: indicates relative to which reference frame we want the orientation. Specify sim_handle_world to retrieve the absolute orientation, sim_handle_inverse to retrieve the inverse of the absolute orientation, sim_handle_parent to retrieve the orientation relative to the object's parent, or an object handle relative to whose reference frame you want the orientation. If this handle is the handle of a joint, then the quaternion relative to the joint's moving frame will be returned (unless is combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe, in which case the quaternion relative to the joint's base frame will be returned).

param.name: quaternion
param.description: the quaternion

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetObjects
function.description: Retrieves object handles. Use this in a loop where index starts at 0 and is incremented to get all object handles in the scene

param.name: index
