param.description: object index. First object is located at index 0

param.name: objectType
return
param.description: handle of the object or -1 if no object is located at that index or in case of an error

function.name: simGetObjectSel
function.description: Retrieves the handles of selected objects

param.name: cnt
param.description: the number of selected objects

param.name: return
param.description: a pointer to the selected objects. The user is in charge of releasing the buffer via simReleaseBuffer

function.name: simGetObjectsInTree
function.description: Retrieves object handles in a given hierarchy tree

param.name: treeBaseHandle
param.description: handle of the object that describes the hierarchy tree, or sim_handle_scene for all objects in the scene.

param.name: objectType
param.description: object type to retrieve or sim_handle_all for any type of object in the tree

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): exclude the tree base from the returned array
bit1 set (2): include in the returned array only the object's first children. If treeBaseHandle is sim_handle_scene, then only parentless objects will be included.

param.name: objectCount
param.description: the number of returned object handles

param.name: return
param.description: a pointer to an array containing object handles, or null in case of an error. The user is in charge of releasing the returned buffer with simReleaseBuffer .

function.name: simGetObjectType
function.description: Retrieves the type of an object

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object

param.name: return

function.name: simGetObjectUid
function.description: Retrieves the unique identifier of an object: throughout a CoppeliaSim session, there won't be two identical unique identifiers. Unique identifiers are however not persistent (i.e. are not saved with the object)

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: object handle

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the unique identifier

function.name: simGetObjectVelocity
function.description: Retrieves the linear and/or angular velocity of an object, in absolute coordinates. The velocity is a measured velocity (i.e. from one simulation step to the next), and is available for all objects in the scene

param.name: objectHandle
param.description:
handle of a scene object . It is highly recommended to add
sim_handleflag_axis
to the object handle, in order to obtain the
regular
angular velocity for the object, i.e. the rotation axis and the rotation velocity that is represented by the axis' length. If
sim_handleflag_axis
is omitted, then a derivative of Euler angles expressed relative to previous' simulation frame will be returned for the angular velocity

param.name: linearVelocity
param.description: pointer to 3 values that will receive the linear velocity. Can be null

param.name: angularVelocity
param.description: pointer to 3 values that will receive the angular velocity. Can be null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetOctreeVoxels
function.description: Retrieves voxel positions from an OC tree

param.name: octreeHandle
param.description: handle of the OC tree

param.name: ptCnt
param.description: a pointer receiving the number of voxels contained in the returned pointer.

param.name: reserved
param.description: reserved for future extensions. Set to null

param.name: return
param.description: null if operation was not successful or if the OC tree doesn't contain any voxels. Otherwise a pointer to the voxel [x y z] positions, relative to the OC tree reference frame

function.name: simGetPage
function.description: Retrieves the current page index (view)

param.name: return
param.description: page index or -1 in case of an error

function.name: simGetPluginInfo
function.description: Returns information about a loaded plugin

param.name: pluginName
param.description: name of the plugin

param.name: infoType
param.description: type of information to retrieve

param.name: stringInfo
param.description: pointer to a string information, in case the information type is for a string. The user is in charge of releasing the string buffer with sim_releaseBuffer

param.name: intInfo
param.description: pointer to an integer information, in case the information type is for an integer.

param.name: return
param.description: 1 in case of success

function.name: simGetPointCloudOptions
function.description: Gets various properties of a point cloud

param.name: pointCloudHandle
param.description: handle of the point cloud

param.name: maxVoxelSize
param.description: maximum size of the OC tree voxels containing points

param.name: maxPtCntPerVoxel
param.description: maximum number of points allowed in a same OC tree voxel

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): points have random colors
bit1 set (2): show OC tree structure
bit2 set (4): reserved. keep unset
bit3 set (8): do not use an OC tree structure. When enabled, point cloud operations are limited, and point clouds will not be collidable , measurable or detectable anymore, but adding points will be much faster
bit4 set (16): color is emissive

param.name: pointSize
param.description: size of the points, in pixels

param.name: reserved
param.description: set to null

param.name: return
param.description: 1 if operation was successful

function.name: simGetPointCloudPoints
function.description: Retrieves point positions from a point cloud

param.name: pointCloudHandle
param.description: handle of the point cloud

param.name: ptCnt
param.description: pointer receiving the number of points contained in the returned pointer.

param.name: reserved
param.description: reserved for future extensions. Set to null

param.name: return
param.description: null if operation was not successful or if the point cloud doesn't contain any points. Otherwise a pointer to the point [x y z] positions, relative to the point cloud reference frame

function.name: simGetQHull
function.description: Retrieves the convex hull mesh from the specified vertices

param.name: inVertices
param.description: pointer to the input vertices (succession of x/y/z values).

param.name: inVerticesL
param.description: number of input vertices times 3.

param.name: verticesOut
param.description: pointer to a pointer to the output vertices. The output vertices are allocated by CoppeliaSim and the user is in charge of releasing the buffer via simReleaseBuffer .

param.name: verticesOutL
param.description: pointer to the number of output vertices times 3.

param.name: indicesOut
param.description: pointer to a pointer to the output indices. The output indices are allocated by CoppeliaSim and the user is in charge of releasing the buffer via simReleaseBuffer .

param.name: indicesOutL
param.description: pointer to the number of output indices (i.e. the number of triangles times 3).

param.name: reserved1
param.description: reserved, set to 0.

param.name: reserved2
param.description: reserved, set to null.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 or 0 if operation was not successful.

function.name: simGetReferencedHandles
function.description: Retrieves a list of custom handles, linking a given scene object to other scene objects

param.name: objectHandle
param.description:
handle of the scene object that stores the list of handles. Can be optionally combined with
sim_handleflag_keeporiginal
, in which case the handles that link to originals are retrieved.

param.name: referencedHandles
param.description: a pointer to a pointer that will be allocated and receive the list of scene object handles. The user is in charge of releasing that buffer with simReleaseBuffer .

param.name: tag
param.description: a tag: only handles stored within that tag are retrieved. null defaults to an empty tag

param.name: reserved
param.description: reserved for future extensions

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error. Otherwise, the number of handles returned.

function.name: simGetScaledImage
function.description: Generates a scaled-up or scaled down version of the input image

param.name: imageIn
param.description: pointer to rgb or rgba values of the input image.

param.name: resolutionIn
param.description: resolution of the input image.

param.name: resolutionOut
param.description: desired resolution of the output image. The values will be replaced by the effective resolution of the output image

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): the input image is rgba, otherwise it is rgb
bit1 set (2): the returned image is rgba, otherwise it is rgb
bit2-3: 0:ignore aspect ratio, 4:keep aspect ratio (the effective resolution of the returned image will be different), 8:keep aspect ratio by expanding (the effective resolution of the returned image will be different)
bit4 set (16): no smooth transformation

param.name: reserved
param.description: bit0 set (1): the input image is rgba, otherwise it is rgb

param.name: return
param.description: null if operation was not successful, otherwise a buffer containing the output image data. The user is in charge of releasing the buffer with simReleaseBuffer .

function.name: simGetScriptHandleEx
function.description: Retrieves the handle of a script.

param.name: scriptType
param.description: the type of the script .

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: the object handle the script is attached to (in case of simulation- or customization scripts. Can be -1.

param.name: scriptName
param.description: the path of the object the script is attached to (in case of simulation- or customization scripts), or the name of the add-on. Can be null.

param.name: return
param.description: handle of the script, or -1 if the script does not exist.

function.name: simGetShapeColor
function.description: Retrieves the color of a shape

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape

param.name: colorName
param.description:
name of a color. If a name is provided, a specific color component will be retrieved (e.g. if a shape is a compound shape. Can be null. If colorName is
@compound
, then rgb data for every component of a compound shape will be returned (in that case make sure to have enough space in
rgbData
, e.g. via the meshes property)

param.name: colorComponent
param.description: color component .

param.name: rgbData
param.description: (output): red, green and blue components of the color (3 values), or the transparency value (1 value)

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful. 0 if the color name was not found in the shape. Otherwise, the operation was successful

function.name: simGetShapeGeomInfo
function.description: Retrieves geometric information related to a shape

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape

param.name: intData
param.description: pointer to 5 integer values: intData[0]: the primitive type of the shape. Undefined if the shape is a compound shape.

param.name: floatData
param.description:
pointer to 4 double values:
floatData[0]: X-size or diameter of the primitive shape. Undefined if the shape is a compound shape or not primitive.
floatData[1]: Y-size of the primitive shape. Undefined if the shape is a compound shape or not primitive.
floatData[2]: Z-size or height of the primitive shape. Undefined if the shape is a compound shape or not primitive.
floatData[3]: Reserved.

param.name: reserved
param.description: set to null

param.name: return
param.description:
-1 in case of an error, otherwise bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): shape is a compound shape
bit1 set (2): shape is primitive
bit2 set (4): shape is convex

function.name: simGetShapeInertia
function.description: Retrieves the inertia information from a shape

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape object

param.name: inertiaMatrix
param.description: (output): inertia matrix (9 values), expressed relative to the shape's reference frame orientation. The matrix is symmetric

param.name: com
param.description: (output): a transformation matrix [0 0 0 P0 0 0 0 P1 0 0 0 P2] indicating the center of mass, expressed relative to the shape's reference frame. The matrix does not contain any rotational part (only the positional values are relevant).

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error

function.name: simGetShapeMass
function.description: Retrieves the mass of a shape

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape object

param.name: mass
param.description: mass of the shape

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error

function.name: simGetShapeMesh
function.description: Retrieves a shape's mesh information

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape

param.name: vertices
param.description: receives the vertices. The user is in charge of destroying the array with simReleaseBuffer . See simExportMesh for a usage example.

param.name: verticesSize
param.description: receives the size of the vertices array. See simExportMesh for a usage example.

param.name: indices
param.description: receives the indices. The user is in charge of destroying the array with simReleaseBuffer . See simExportMesh for a usage example.

param.name: indicesSize
param.description: receives the size of the indice array. See simExportMesh for a usage example.

param.name: normals
param.description: receives the normals (3 times the size of indicesSize). The user is in charge of destroying the array with simReleaseBuffer . Can be null.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetShapeTextureId
function.description: Retrieves the texture ID of a texture that is applied to a specific shape

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape.

param.name: return
param.description: The texture ID, or -1 if the texture does not exist or in case of an error

function.name: simGetShapeViz
function.description: Retrieves a shape's visual information.

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape. Can be combined with sim_handleflag_extended in order to retrieve extended data

param.name: index
param.description: 0-based index of the shape element to retrieve (compound shapes contain more than one shape element)

param.name: info
param.description:
visual information about the shape:
vertices : array of vertices, relative to the shape's reference frame. The user is in charge of releasing this buffer with simReleaseBuffer if the return value is > 0.
verticesSize : the size of the vertices array
indices : array of indices. The user is in charge of releasing this buffer with simReleaseBuffer if the return value is > 0.
indicesSize : the size of the indices array
normals : array of normals, relative to the shape's reference frame. There are 3*indicesSize normals, i.e. 3 normals per triangle. The user is in charge of releasing this buffer with simReleaseBuffer if the return value is > 0.
shadingAngle : the gouraud shading angle
colors[9] : array of colors: RGB for ambient-diffuse, specular, and emission.
transparency : transparency value (0.0 = opaque, 1.0 = fully transparent). To have this field written, combine shapeHandle with sim_handleflag_extended
options : bit-coded options (to have this field written, combine shapeHandle with sim_handleflag_extended):
bit0 set (1): backfaces are culled
texture : the RGBA texture (32bit/pixel). The user is in charge of releasing this buffer with simReleaseBuffer if the return value is > 1.
textureId : a texture id (to identify textures shared among several shapes)
textureRes[2] : the resolution of the texture
textureCoords : the texture coordinates. There are 3*indicesSize texture coordinates, i.e. 3 coordinates per triangle. The user is in charge of releasing this buffer with simReleaseBuffer if the return value is > 1.
textureApplyMode : 0=modulate, 1=decal, 2=add
textureOptions : bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): repeat U
bit1 set (2): repeat V
bit2 set (4): interpolate colors
bit3 set (8): wireframe

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, 0 if there is no shape element at the given indexm, 1 if the shape element does not contain any texture, 2 if the shape element contains a texture

function.name: simGetSimulationState
function.description: Retrieves current simulation state

param.name: return

function.name: simGetSimulationTime
function.description: Retrieves the current simulation time

param.name: return
param.description: negative value (-1.0) if operation not successful, otherwise the simulation time

function.name: simGetSimulationTimeStep
function.description: Retrieves the simulation time step (the simulation time (i.e. not real-time) that passes at each main script simulation pass). This value might not be constant for a given simulation.

param.name: return
param.description: negative value (-1.0) if operation not successful, otherwise the simulation time step

function.name: simGetSimulatorMessage
function.description: Retrieves and removes the next message in the C/C++ or Lua message queues. Use this in a while-loop until all messages have been extracted. While the C/C++ interface has one single message queue, each Lua script has its own message queue. The C/C++ version of this function should only be called from the CoppeliaSim client application. A given message queue cannot hold more than 64 messages, unread messages will be discarded.

param.name: messageID
auxiliaryData
param.description: array of 4 integers that can describe the returned message in more details

param.name: returnedDataSize
param.description: size of the returned buffer

param.name: return
param.description: null if no buffer was returned, otherwise a buffer that should be released with simReleaseBuffer

function.name: simGetTextureId
function.description: Retrieves the texture ID of a specific texture

param.name: textureName
param.description: name of the texture ID to be retrieved.

param.name: resolution
param.description: pointer to 2 integer values representing the resolution of the texture. Can be null.

param.name: return
param.description: The texture ID, or -1 if the texture does not exist or in case of an error

function.name: simGetVelocity
function.description: Retrieves the linear and/or angular velocity of the center of mass of a non-static shape. Data is provided by the selected physics engine

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of a dynamically enabled shape

param.name: linearVelocity
param.description: pointer to 3 values that will receive the linear velocity in absolute coordinates. Can be null

param.name: angularVelocity
param.description: pointer to 3 values that will receive the angular velocity in absolute coordinates. The length of the vector represents the rotation speed, and the vector direction represents the rotation axis passing through the center of mass of the shape. Can be null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetVisionSensorDepth
function.description: Reads the depth buffer of a vision sensor. The returned data doesn't make sense if sim_handleVisionSensor wasn't called previously

param.name: sensorHandle
param.description: handle of the vision sensor

param.name: options
param.description:
options, bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): the returned values indicate distances in meters, otherwise return values are in the range of 0.0 - 1.0 (0.0 = closest to sensor (i.e. close clipping plane), 1.0 = farthest from sensor (i.e. far clipping plane))

param.name: pos
param.description: position of the depth buffer portion to retrieve. Can be null, which defaults to [0 0]

param.name: size
param.description: size of the depth buffer portion to retrieve. Can be null, which defaults to [0 0], which corresponds to [resolutionX resolutionY]

param.name: resolution
param.description: the returned vision sensor resolution. Can be null

param.name: return
param.description: the depth buffer or null in case of an error. The user is in charge of releasing the returned buffer with simReleaseBuffer

function.name: simGetVisionSensorImg
function.description: Reads the image of a vision sensor. The returned data doesn't make sense if sim_handleVisionSensor wasn't called previously

param.name: sensorHandle
param.description: handle of the vision sensor

param.name: options
param.description:
options, bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): the returned image will by a greyscale image (1 byte per pixel), otherwise an RGB image
bit1 set (2): the returned image will have one additional byte per pixel, as alpha channel (RGBA or greyscale+A)

param.name: rgbaCutOff
param.description: when an RGBA image is returned, the alpha component will be 255 for all depth values below rgbaCutOff, and 0 for all depth values above rgbaCutOff. 0 corresponds to the near clipping plane, 1 to the far clipping plane

param.name: pos
param.description: position of the image portion to retrieve. Can be null, which defaults to [0 0]

param.name: size
param.description: size of the image portion to retrieve. Can be null which defaults to [0 0], which corresponds to [resolutionX resolutionY]

param.name: resolution
param.description: the returned vision sensor resolution. Can be null

param.name: return
param.description: the image buffer or null in case of an error. The user is in charge of releasing the returned buffer with simReleaseBuffer

function.name: simGetVisionSensorRes
function.description: Returns the resolution of the vision sensor

param.name: sensorHandle
param.description: handle of the vision sensor

param.name: resolution
param.description: 2 values for the x and y component

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGroupShapes
function.description: Groups (or merges) several shapes into a compound shape (or simple shape )

param.name: shapeHandles
param.description: the handles of the shapes to be grouped or merged. The last handle represents the shape that will accept the other shapes.

param.name: shapeCount
param.description: the size of the array. A negative number indicates that we want to merge the shapes instead of grouping them.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful. Otherwise the handle of the resulting compound shape, which is the last shape in shapeHandles.

function.name: simHandleDynamics
function.description: Handles the dynamics functionality in a scene

param.name: deltaTime
param.description: simulation time step, i.e. simGetSimulationTimeStep ()

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful. 0 if the sepcified physics engine could not be found, otherwise, the number of calculation steps performed by the physics engine.

function.name: simHandleGraph
function.description: Handles a graph object (i.e. records another value for each curve, given that such value was provided via sim_setGraphStreamValue

param.name: graphHandle
param.description: handle of the graph object.

param.name: time
param.description: the time where next value will be recorded at, usually the simulation time.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simHandleProximitySensor
function.description: Handles (performs sensing, etc. of) a registered proximity sensor object

param.name: sensorHandle
param.description: handle of a proximity sensor object or sim_handle_all or sim_handle_all_except_explicit. (sim_handle_all will handle all proximity sensor objects, while sim_handle_all_except_explicit will only handle those that are not marked as "explicit handling")

param.name: detectedPoint
param.description: coordinates of the closest detected point (x, y and z: detectedPoint[0]-detectedPoint[2]) relative to the sensor reference frame, and distance to the detected point (1 value: detectedPoint[3]). Can be null

param.name: detectedObjectHandle
param.description: handle of the object that was detected. Can be null

param.name: detectedSurfaceNormalVector
param.description: normal vector (normalized) of the detected surface. Relative to the sensor reference frame. Can be null

param.name: return
param.description: 0 if nothing was detected, -1 in case of an error.

function.name: simHandleVisionSensor
function.description: Handles (performs sensing, etc. of) a vision sensor object. It (1) clear previous computed image processing data, (2) reads an image and (3) performs image processing via the vision callback functions (if the vision sensor is using an external input only (1) is performed)

param.name: visionSensorHandle
param.description: handle of a vision sensor object or sim_handle_all or sim_handle_all_except_explicit. (sim_handle_all will handle all vision sensor objects, while sim_handle_all_except_explicit will only handle those that are not marked as "explicit handling")

param.name: auxValues
param.description: by default CoppeliaSim returns one packet of 15 auxiliary values: the minimum of [intensity red green blue depth], the maximum of [intensity red green blue depth], and the average of [intensity red green blue depth]. If the vision callback function returns additional values, then they are appended as packets to the first packet. AuxValues can be null. The user is in charge of releasing the auxValues buffer with simReleaseBuffer (*auxValues). If visionSensorHandle is sim_handle_all or sim_handle_all_except_explicit, nothing is returned in auxValues.

param.name: auxValuesCount
param.description: contains information about the number of auxiliary value packets and packet sizes returned in auxValues. The first value is the number of packets, the second is the size of packet1, the third is the size of packet2, etc. Can be null if auxValues is also null. The user is in charge of releasing the auxValuesCount buffer with simReleaseBuffer (*auxValuesCount).

param.name: return
param.description: number of detections (number of vision sensors that triggered a detection), -1 in case of an error

function.name: simImportMesh
function.description: Imports a mesh from a file

param.name: fileformat
param.description: set to 0. Fileformat is automatically detected

param.name: pathAndFilename
param.description: the location of the file to import.

param.name: options
param.description: bit-coded: bit0 set (1): keep identical vertices, bit7 set (128): ignore up-vector coded in fileformat

param.name: identicalVerticeTolerance
param.description: has no effect. set to zero

param.name: scalingFactor
param.description: the scaling factor to apply to the imported vertices

param.name: vertices
param.description: an array to vertice arrays. The import operation may generate several meshes depending on the fileformat. The user is in charge of releasing the memory. See the example below

param.name: verticesSizes
param.description: an array indicating the individual vertice array sizes. The user is in charge of releasing the memory. See the example below

param.name: indices
param.description: an array to indice arrays. The import operation may generate several meshes depending on the fileformat. The user is in charge of releasing the memory. Can be null. See the example below

param.name: indicesSizes
param.description: an array indicating the individual indice array sizes. The user is in charge of releasing the memory. Can be null if indices is also null. See the example below

param.name: reserved
param.description: reserved for future extensions. Keep at null.

param.name: names
param.description: not used anymore. Set to null

param.name: return
param.description: Number of imported meshes, or 0 or -1 if the operation was not successful

function.name: simImportShape
function.description: Imports a shape from a file (first imports meshes, then groups/merges them into a shape)

param.name: fileformat
param.description: set to 0. Fileformat is automatically detected

param.name: pathAndFilename
param.description: the location of the file to import.

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): keep identical vertices
bit1 set (2): reserced. keep at 0.
bit2 set (4): reserved. keep at 0.
bit3 set (8): do not preserve colors
bit4 set (16): tries to preserve textures.
bit5 set (32): realigns the shape's bounding box with the world.
bit7 set (128): ignore up-vector coded in fileformat.

param.name: identicalVerticeTolerance
param.description: not used. Set to 0

param.name: scalingFactor
param.description: the scaling factor to apply to the imported vertices

param.name: return
param.description: The handle of the imported shape, or -1 if the operation was not successful

function.name: simInitScript
function.description: Initializes/reinitializes a script. Operates in an asynchronous way when called upon the calling script

param.name: scriptHandle
param.description: handle of the script to initialize/reinitialize

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error

function.name: simInsertObjectIntoOctree
function.description: Inserts an object into an OC tree , as voxels. Each voxel will store a color and a tag value

param.name: octreeHandle
param.description: handle of the OC tree

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object to insert. Only potentially collidable objects are supported

param.name: options
param.description: reserved. Set to 0

param.name: color
param.description: pointer to one RGB triple, specifying the red, green and blue color components (0-255). Can be null.

param.name: tag
param.description: uint32 value, which is user-defined.

param.name: reserved
param.description: reserved for future extensions. Set to null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the total number of voxels in the OC tree

function.name: simInsertObjectIntoPointCloud
function.description: Inserts an object into a point cloud , as points

param.name: pointCloudHandle
param.description: handle of the point cloud

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object to insert. Only potentially collidable objects are supported

param.name: options
param.description: reserved. Set to 0

param.name: gridSize
param.description: when a shape is inserted, it will first be converted to an OC tree with a given grid or voxel size.

param.name: color
param.description: pointer to one RGB triple, specifying the red, green and blue color components (0-255). Can be null.

param.name: optionalValues
param.description:
can be used to specify additional parameters, or set to null for default parameter values:
((int*)optionalValues)[0]: integer value that is bit coded. Each bit indicates which additional parameter will be taken into account:
((float*)optionalValues)[1]: duplicateTolerance : a minimum distance tolerance value that is used to avoid duplicate points. To have this parameter taken into account, set bit0 to 1 in ((int*)optionalValues)[0]. Point insertion is slower when the duplicate tolerance is > then 0.0

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the total number of points in the point cloud

function.name: simInsertPointsIntoPointCloud
function.description: Inserts points into a point cloud

param.name: pointCloudHandle
param.description: the handle of the point cloud

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): specified points are relative to the point cloud reference frame, otherwise they are relative to the world reference frame
bit1 set (2): the color array contains one RGB triple per point. Otherwise it contains a single RGB triple

param.name: pts
param.description: a pointer to the point positions specified as x/y/z coordinates

param.name: ptCnt
param.description: the number of point coordinates contained in pts

param.name: color
param.description: a pointer to one or several RGB triplets, specifying the red, green and blue color components (0-255). Can be null

param.name: optionalValues
param.description:
can be used to specify additional parameters, or set to null for default parameter values:
((int*)optionalValues)[0]: an integer value that is bit coded. Each bit indicates which additional parameter will be taken into account:
((float*)optionalValues)[1]: duplicateTolerance: a minimum distance tolerance value that is used to avoid duplicate points. To have this parameter taken into account, set bit0 to 1 in ((int*)optionalValues)[0]. Point insertion is slower when the duplicate tolerance is > then 0.0

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the total number of points in the point cloud

function.name: simInsertVoxelsIntoOctree
function.description: Inserts voxels into an OC tree . Each voxel will store a color and a tag value

param.name: octreeHandle
param.description: handle of the OC tree

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): specified points are relative to the OC tree reference frame, otherwise they are relative to the world reference frame
bit1 set (2): the color array contains one RGB triple per point, and the tag array contains one value per point. Otherwise it the color array contains a single RGB triple, and the tag array contains a single value.

param.name: pts
param.description: pointer to the voxel positions specified as x/y/z coordinates

param.name: ptCnt
param.description: number of point coordinates contained in pts

param.name: color
param.description: pointer to one or several RGB triplets, specifying the red, green and blue color components (0-255). Can be null

param.name: tag
param.description: pointer to one or several uint32 values, which are user-defined values. Can be null, and should be null if color is null

param.name: reserved
param.description: pointer to one or several RGB triplets, specifying the red, green and blue color components (0-255). Can be null.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the total number of voxels in the OC tree

function.name: simIntersectPointsWithPointCloud
function.description: Removes points from a point cloud , that do not intersect with the provided points (i.e. the result in the point cloud will be the intersection between the two sets of points). When a point cloud doesn't use an OC tree calculation structure, then this operation cannot be performed

param.name: pointCloudHandle
param.description: handle of the point cloud

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): specified points are relative to the point cloud reference frame, otherwise they are relative to the world reference frame

param.name: pts
param.description: pointer to the point positions specified as x/y/z coordinates

param.name: ptCnt
param.description: number of point coordinates contained in pts

param.name: tolerance
param.description: distance used as a tolerance value

param.name: reserved
param.description: set to null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the total number of points in the point cloud

function.name: simInvertMatrix
function.description: Inverts a transformation matrix

param.name: matrix
param.description: input/output matrix (array of 12 values [Vx0 Vy0 Vz0 P0 Vx1 Vy1 Vz1 P1 Vx2 Vy2 Vz2 P2])

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simInvertPose
function.description: Inverts a pose

param.name: pose
param.description: input/output pose (array of 7 values [x y z qx qy qz qw])

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simIsDynamicallyEnabled
function.description: Checks whether a scene object is dynamically enabled, i.e. is being handled and simulated by the physics engine. Note that until the physics engine has parsed the scene in the first simulation step (i.e. the first time sim_handleDynamics is called), no object will be dynamically enabled

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the scene object

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, 0 if the object is not dynamically enabled, 1 if the object is dynamically enabled.

function.name: simIsHandle
function.description: Checks whether a general object handle is still valid. When a general object is destroyed (e.g. programmatically or via the user interface), then its related handle is not valid anymore and will trigger an error when used. Use this function to avoid triggering an error

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object

param.name: reserved
param.description: set to -1

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, 0 if the handle is not valid anymore, or 1 if the handle is still valid.

function.name: simLoadImage
function.description: Loads an image

param.name: resolution
param.description: pointer that will accept the image resolution.

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 is set (1): the returned image is rgba, otherwise it is rgb

param.name: filenameOrBuffer
param.description: name of the file to read. The file extension indicates the format. If you wish to load an image from a memory buffer, then have filename point to that memory buffer

param.name: reserved
param.description: set to null

param.name: return
param.description: null if operation was not successful, otherwise a buffer containing the image data. The user is in charge of releasing the buffer with simReleaseBuffer .

function.name: simSaveImage
function.description: Saves an image to file or to memory

param.name: image
param.description: a pointer to rgb, rgba or greyscale values

param.name: resolution
param.description: the x/y resolution of the provided image

param.name: options
param.description: bit-coded. If bit0 and bit1 represent the format of the provided image (0=rgb, 1=rgba, 2=greyscale)

param.name: filename
param.description: the name of the file to write. The file extension indicates the format

param.name: quality
param.description: the quality of the written image: 0 for best compression, 100 for largest file. Use -1 for default behaviour

param.name: reserved
param.description: reserved for future extension. Set to null

param.name: return
param.description: 1 if operation was successful.

function.name: simLoadModel
function.description: Loads a previously saved model, and selects it

param.name: filename
param.description:
model filename. The filename extension is required (
ttm
or
simmodel.xml
).

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful. Otherwise the handle of the model base object.

function.name: simLoadScene
function.description: Loads a scene

param.name: filename
param.description: scene filename, including extension. An empty string loads the default scene. By default, the current scene is overwritten (append "@keepCurrent" to the filename to preserve current scene)

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simMatrixToPose
function.description: Converts a transformation matrix to a pose

param.name: matrixIn
param.description: input matrix (array of 12 values [Vx0 Vy0 Vz0 P0 Vx1 Vy1 Vz1 P1 Vx2 Vy2 Vz2 P2])

param.name: poseOut
param.description: output pose (array of 7 values [x y z qx qy qz qw])

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simModuleEntry
function.description: Creates, modifies or destroys module menu entries. Those are user selectable items located in [Menu bar > Modules]. When selected, the corresponding script will have its sysCall_moduleEntry callback function triggered, or sim_message_eventcallback_moduleentry triggered

param.name: handle
param.description: handle of a module menu entry, or -1 to create one.

param.name: label
param.description:
label of the module entry. If handle is -1, then a path can be specified, in order to build sub-menus, e.g. "Menu
Submenu1
Submenu2
Label". Can be null if handle is not -1.

param.name: state
param.description:
Bit-coded (is ignored if set to -1, -2 destroys an existing item):
bit0 is set (1): the item is enabled
bit1 is set (2): the item is checked
bit2 is set (4): the item is checkable

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the handle of the module menu entry.

function.name: simMultiplyMatrices
function.description: Multiplies two transformation matrices

param.name: matrixIn1
param.description: first input matrix (array of 12 values [Vx0 Vy0 Vz0 P0 Vx1 Vy1 Vz1 P1 Vx2 Vy2 Vz2 P2])

param.name: matrixIn2
param.description: second input matrix (array of 12 values [Vx0 Vy0 Vz0 P0 Vx1 Vy1 Vz1 P1 Vx2 Vy2 Vz2 P2])

param.name: matrixOut
param.description: output matrix (array of 12 values [Vx0 Vy0 Vz0 P0 Vx1 Vy1 Vz1 P1 Vx2 Vy2 Vz2 P2])

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simMultiplyPoses
function.description: Multiplies two poses

param.name: poseIn1
param.description: first input pose (array of 7 values [x y z qx qy qz qw])

param.name: poseIn2
param.description: second input pose (array of 7 values [x y z qx qy qz qw])

param.name: poseOut
param.description: output pose (array of 7 values [x y z qx qy qz qw])

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simOpenTextEditor
function.description: Opens a text edition window. The C version of this function opens a modal window

param.name: initText
param.description: pointer to the initial text to be displayed.

param.name: xml
param.description: pointer to an XML description of the text editor's properties. Can be null for default properties

param.name: various
param.description: optional pointer holding the window size (various[0]-various[1]) and position (various[2],various[3])

param.name: return
param.description: null in case of an error. Otherwise a pointer to the text. The user is in charge of releasing the returned buffer with simReleaseBuffer .

function.name: simPackTable
function.description: Packs a table into a buffer. Functions and other non-serializable items will be packed as string or None/nil values. You can also use sim_packTable to quickly compare two tables

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: stack handle obtained with simCreateStack . There must be a table located at the top of the stack.

param.name: bufferSize
param.description: size of the returned buffer.

param.name: return
param.description: null in case of an error, otherwise a data buffer. The user is in charge of releasing the returned buffer with simReleaseBuffer .

function.name: simPauseSimulation
function.description: Requests a pause of a simulation

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error, 0 if the operation could not be performed. >0 in case of success.

function.name: simPoseToMatrix
function.description: Converts a pose to a transformation matrix

param.name: poseIn
param.description: the input pose (array of 7 values [x y z qx qy qz qw])

param.name: matrixOut
param.description: the output matrix (array of 12 values [Vx0 Vy0 Vz0 P0 Vx1 Vy1 Vz1 P1 Vx2 Vy2 Vz2 P2])

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simQuitSimulator
function.description: Triggers a quit signal after which the application eventually ends

param.name: ignoredArg
param.description: set to true.

function.name: simReadForceSensor
function.description: Reads the force and torque applied to a force sensor (filtered values are read)

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object (must be a force sensor). Can be combined with sim_handleflag_rawvalue (simply add sim_handleflag_rawvalue to objectHandle), if you wish to access the raw values generated by each individual dynamic simulation step (by default, there are 10 dynamic simulation steps for each simulation step). Raw values can only be accessed from inside a callback function triggered by the physics engine.

param.name: forceVector
param.description: pointer to 3 values (applied forces along the sensor's x, y and z-axes). Can be null

param.name: torqueVector
param.description: pointer to 3 values (applied torques about the sensor's x, y and z-axes). Can be null

param.name: return
param.description:
-1 in case of an error, otherwise bit-coded:
bit 0 set (1): force and torque data is available, otherwise it is not (yet) available (e.g. when not enough values are present for the filter)

function.name: simReadProximitySensor
function.description: Reads the state of a proximity sensor. This function doesn't perform detection, it merely reads the result from a previous call to sim_handleProximitySensor

param.name: sensorHandle
param.description: handle of a proximity sensor object

param.name: detectedPoint
param.description: coordinates of the closest detected point (x, y and z: detectedPoint[0]-detectedPoint[2]) relative to the sensor reference frame, and distance to the detected point (1 value: detectedPoint[3]). Can be null

param.name: detectedObjectHandle
param.description: handle of the object that was detected. Can be null

param.name: detectedSurfaceNormalVector
param.description: normal vector (normalized) of the detected surface. Relative to the sensor reference frame. Can be null

param.name: return
param.description: detection state (0 or 1), or -1 in case of an error.

function.name: simReadTexture
function.description: Retrieves the RGB data (or a portion of it) related to a specific texture

param.name: textureId
param.description: ID of the texture. See also simGetTextureId .

param.name: options
param.description: reserved for future functionality. Set to zero.

param.name: posX
param.description: x/y position of the texture portion to retrieve. Set to [0 0] to retrieve the full texture

param.name: posY
param.description: x/y position of the texture portion to retrieve. Set to [0 0] to retrieve the full texture

param.name: sizeX
param.description: x/y size of the texture portion to retrieve. Set to [0 0] to retrieve the full texture

param.name: sizeY
param.description: x/y size of the texture portion to retrieve. Set to [0 0] to retrieve the full texture

param.name: return
param.description: The texture data, or null in case of an error. The texture data contains RGB values between 0-255 (3 bytes per pixel). The user is in charge of releasing the returned buffer with simReleaseBuffer .

function.name: simReadVisionSensor
function.description: Reads the state of a vision sensor. This function doesn't perform detection, it merely reads the result from a previous call to sim_handleVisionSensor

param.name: visionSensorHandle
param.description: handle of a vision sensor object

param.name: auxValues
param.description: by default CoppeliaSim returns one packet of 15 auxiliary values: the minimum of [intensity red green blue depth], the maximum of [intensity red green blue depth], and the average of [intensity red green blue depth]. Additional packets can be appended in the vision callback function . AuxValues can be null. The user is in charge of releasing the auxValues buffer with simReleaseBuffer (*auxValues).

param.name: auxValuesCount
param.description: contains information about the number of auxiliary value packets and packet sizes returned in auxValues. The first value is the number of packets, the second is the size of packet1, the third is the size of packet2, etc. Can be null if auxValues is also null. The user is in charge of releasing the auxValuesCount buffer with simReleaseBuffer (*auxValuesCount).

param.name: return
param.description: detection state (0 or 1), or -1 in case of an error, or if simHandleVisionSensor was never called, or if simResetVisionSensor was previously called.

function.name: simRefreshDialogs
function.description: Refreshes CoppeliaSim's internal dialogs. Calling sim_refreshDialogs will not trigger a sim_message_eventcallback_refreshdialogs message

param.name: refreshDegree
param.description: refresh degree (0=light, 1=medium, 2=full)

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simRegisterScriptCallbackFunction
function.description:
Registers a custom script function, that calls back a c/c++ function. This function is useful for plugins that wish to provide their own or customized script functions. Should be called inside of simInit entry point
Data exchange between a script and the plugin happens via a stack . Reading and writing arguments from/to the stack gives you a maximum of flexibility, and you wil be able to exchange also complex data structures. But it can also be tedious, if your data structures are anyway relatively simple. In that case you can use the helper classes
CScriptFunctionData
and
CScriptFunctionDataItem
located in
programming/include
: they will greatly simplify the task.
Use following 4 functions in the helper class:
readDataFromStack
,
getInDataPtr
,
pushOutData
and
writeDataToStack

param.name: func
param.description: name of the function, without namespace

param.name: reserved
param.description: set to null

param.name: cb)
param.description: callback address that is called when the function is called from Lua. See further down for a simple way to call above function, using a helper class. The callback's first argument is a SScriptCallBack structure that holds:

param.name: return
param.description: 1 if function was registered, 0 if function was replaced (when that function name already existed), -1 in case of an error

function.name: simRegisterScriptFuncHook
function.description: Registers a script function hook for callback functions , that will be executed before or after the specified function. Calling this function a second time with the same arguments will unregister a previous hook

param.name: scriptHandle
param.description: handle of the script

param.name: funcToHook
param.description: function to hook

param.name: userFunc
param.description: user function

param.name: execBefore
param.description: if true, the user function is called before the function to hook. Otherwise it is called after

param.name: options
param.description: set to 0

param.name: return
param.description: 1 if the hook registration was successful, 0 if a previous hook was removed, and -1 in case of an error

function.name: simRegisterScriptVariable
function.description: Registers a script variable. Should be called inside of simInit entry point

param.name: var
param.description: name of the variable, without namespace

param.name: val
param.description: value of the variable. Can be null, in which case the value of the variable will be the top item of the provided stack

param.name: stackhandle
param.description:
a stack handle obtained with simCreateStack . Set to 0 if
varValue
is not null. If a stack is provided, it will be released at a later point by CoppeliaSim.

param.name: return
param.description: 1 if the variable was registered, 0 if the variable was replaced because it already existed, -1 in case of an error

function.name: simRelocateShapeFrame
function.description: Repositions and reorients the reference frame of a shape, while keeping the mesh in place. The shape's inertia properties are unaffected.

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape

param.name: pose
param.description: pose for the shape's reference frame. The pose is expressed as [x y z qx qy qz qw]. A null pose or a pose containing only zeros will relocate the frame to the center of the mesh's bounding box

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful. 0 if the frame could not be relocated (the frame of primitive shapes cannot be relocated), otherwise 1.

function.name: simRemoveModel
function.description: Removes a model from the scene

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the model (i.e. object tagged as model) to remove. A negative value indicates a delayed removal (in that case this argument should be -objectHandle -1

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the number of removed objects (a model might contain several objects)

function.name: simRemoveObjects
function.description: Removes one or several objects from the scene

param.name: objectHandles
param.description: handles of the objects to remove

param.name: count
param.description: number of objects to remove. Negative value indicates a delayed removal

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simRemovePointsFromPointCloud
function.description: Removes points from a point cloud . When a point cloud doesn't use an OC tree calculation structure, then individual points cannot be removed, only all points can be removed in that case

param.name: pointCloudHandle
param.description: handle of the point cloud

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): specified points are relative to the point cloud reference frame, otherwise they are relative to the world reference frame

param.name: pts
param.description: pointer to the point positions specified as x/y/z coordinates. Set to null to remove all points

param.name: ptCnt
param.description: number of point coordinates contained in pts

param.name: tolerance
param.description: distance used as a tolerance value

param.name: reserved
param.description: distance used as a tolerance value

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the total number of points in the point cloud

function.name: simRemoveVoxelsFromOctree
function.description: Removes voxels from an OC tree

param.name: octreeHandle
param.description: handle of the OC tree

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): specified points are relative to the OC tree reference frame, otherwise they are relative to the world reference frame

param.name: pts
param.description: pointer to the voxel positions specified as x/y/z coordinates. Set to null to remove all voxels

param.name: ptCnt
param.description: number of point coordinates contained in pts

param.name: reserved
param.description: set to null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the total number of voxels in the OC tree

function.name: simResetDynamicObject
function.description: Dynamically resets an object that is dynamically simulated. This means that the object representation in the dynamics engine is removed, and added again. This can be useful when the set-up of a dynamically simulated chain needs to be modified during simulation (e.g. joint or shape attachement position/orientation changed)

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object. Can be combined with sim_handleflag_model , if you wish to reset all objects in a model (where objectHandle would be the model base).

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simResetGraph
function.description: Resets a graph object (i.e. clears all its data streams)

param.name: graphHandle
param.description: handle of the graph object

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simResetProximitySensor
function.description: Clears the detection state, detection color, detection segments, etc. of a proximity sensor object

param.name: sensorHandle
param.description: handle of the proximity sensor object or sim_handle_all or sim_handle_all_except_explicit. (sim_handle_all will reset all proximity sensor objects, while sim_handle_all_except_explicit will only reset those that are not marked as "explicit handling")

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simResetScript
function.description: Resets a script interpreter linked to a specific script.

param.name: scriptHandle
param.description: handle of the script to reset or sim_handle_all to reset all scripts

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simResetVisionSensor
function.description: Clears the detection state, etc. of a proximity sensor object

param.name: sensorHandle
param.description: handle of the vision sensor object or sim_handle_all or sim_handle_all_except_explicit. (sim_handle_all will reset all vision sensor objects, while sim_handle_all_except_explicit will only reset those that are not marked as "explicit handling")

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simRuckigPos
function.description: Executes a call to the Ruckig online trajectory generator . The Ruckig online trajectory generator provides instantaneous trajectory generation capabilities for motion control systems. This function prepares a position-based trajectory generation object, that can then be calculated with sim_ruckigStep . When this object is not needed anymore, remove it with sim_ruckigRemove

param.name: dofs
param.description: number of degrees of freedom (n).

param.name: baseCycleTime
param.description: smallest expected cycle time. The cycle time should always be a multiple of baseCycleTime. Use a value of 0.0001 (0.1ms).

param.name: flags
param.description: Ruckig flags . -1 for default flags.

param.name: currentPos
param.description: current position (one value for each DoF)

param.name: currentVel
param.description: current velocity (one value for each DoF)

param.name: currentAccel
param.description: current acceleration (one value for each DoF)

param.name: maxVel
param.description: maximum allowed velocity (one value for each DoF, i.e. [maxV_1 ... maxV_n]). If sim_ruckig_minvel is specified in flags, then maxVel should contain [maxV_1 ... maxV_n minV_1 ... minV_n]

param.name: maxAccel
param.description: maximum allowed acceleration (one value for each DoF, i.e. [maxA_1 ... maxA_n]). If sim_ruckig_minaccel is specified in flags, then maxAccel should contain [maxA_1 ... maxA_n minA_1 ... minA_n]

param.name: maxJerk
param.description: maximum allowed jerk (one value for each DoF)

param.name: selection
param.description: selection vector (one value for each DoF). For a default behaviour, fill the vector with non-zero values.

param.name: targetPos
param.description: target position (one value for each DoF)

param.name: targetVel
param.description: target velocity (one value for each DoF)

param.name: reserved1
param.description: reserved. Set to null

param.name: reserved2l
param.description: reserved. Set to null

param.name: return
param.description: A negative value in case of an error, otherwise the handle of the created object

function.name: simRuckigRemove
function.description: Removes an object previously created via sim_ruckigPos or sim_ruckigVel .

param.name: handle
param.description: handle of the object created via simRuckigPos or simRuckigVel .

param.name: return
param.description: 1 in case of success

function.name: simRuckigStep
function.description: Executes a call to the Ruckig online trajectory generator . The Ruckig online trajectory generator provides instantaneous trajectory generation capabilities for motion control systems. This function steps forward a trajectory generation algorithm previously prepared via sim_ruckigPos or sim_ruckigVel

param.name: handle
param.description: handle of the object created via simRuckigPos or simRuckigVel .

param.name: cycleTime
param.description: cycle time or simulation step. Should always be a multiple of the base cycle time

param.name: newPos
param.description: new position

param.name: newVel
param.description: new velocity

param.name: newAccel
param.description: new acceleration

param.name: syncTime
param.description: synchronization time (output). This is the time needed to reach the desired state. This time does not include the cycle time of the current call to sim_RuckigStep)

param.name: reserved1
param.description: reserved. Set to null

param.name: reserved2
param.description: reserved. Set to null

param.name: return
param.description:
-1 or -2 in case of an immediate error, otherwise the return value of the update function in the motion library:
1: Finished (final state reached)
0: Working (final state not yet reached)
-100: ErrorInvalidInput
-101: ErrorTrajectoryDuration
-110: ErrorExecutionTimeCalculation
-111: ErrorSynchronizationCalculation

function.name: simRuckigVel
function.description: Executes a call to the Ruckig online trajectory generator . The Ruckig online trajectory generator provides instantaneous trajectory generation capabilities for motion control systems. This function prepares a velocity-based trajectory generation object, that can then be calculated with sim_ruckigStep . When this object is not needed anymore, remove it with sim_ruckigRemove

param.name: dofs
param.description: number of degrees of freedom (n).

param.name: baseCycleTime
param.description: smallest expected cycle time. The cycle time should always be a multiple of baseCycleTime. Use a value of 0.0001 (0.1ms).

param.name: flags
param.description: Ruckig flags . -1 for default flags.

param.name: currentPos
param.description: current position (one value for each DoF)

param.name: currentVel
param.description: current velocity (one value for each DoF)

param.name: currentAccel
param.description: current acceleration (one value for each DoF)

param.name: maxAccel
param.description: maximum allowed acceleration (one value for each DoF, i.e. [maxA_1 ... maxA_n]). If sim_ruckig_minaccel is specified in flags, then maxAccel should contain [maxA_1 ... maxA_n minA_1 ... minA_n]

param.name: maxJerk
param.description: maximum allowed jerk (one value for each DoF)

param.name: selection
param.description: selection vector (one value for each DoF). For a default behaviour, fill the vector with non-zero values.

param.name: targetVel
param.description: target velocity (one value for each DoF)

param.name: reserved1
param.description: reserved. Set to null

param.name: reserved2
param.description: reserved. Set to null

param.name: return
param.description: A negative value in case of an error, otherwise the handle of the created object

function.name: simSaveModel
function.description: Saves a model (an object marked as "Object is model base" and all other objects in its hierarchy tree). Any existing file with same name will be overwritten

param.name: baseOfModelHandle
param.description: handle of an object marked as "Object is model base"

param.name: filename
param.description:
model filename. The filename extension is required (
ttm
, or
simmodel.xml
)

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSaveScene
function.description: Saves a scene. Any existing file with same name will be overwritten

param.name: filename
param.description: scene filename. The filename extension is required

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simScaleObject
function.description: Scales specified objects in a non-isometric fashion, if possible. Only non-compound shapes can be non-isometrically scaled. Some primitive shapes can have some constraints between their axes

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object to scale

param.name: xScale
param.description: scaling factors along the object's x-axis

param.name: yScale
param.description: scaling factors along the object's y-axis

param.name: zScale
param.description: scaling factors along the object's z-axis

param.name: options
param.description: keep at 0

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simScaleObjects
function.description: Scales specified objects. All related values are automatically scaled appropriately (e.g. masses, forces, etc.)

param.name: objectHandles
param.description: array containing the handles of the objects to scale. If an object is a model base, all its child objects will also be scaled.

param.name: objectCount
param.description: number of handles in the objectHandles array.

param.name: scalingFactor
param.description: scaling factor

param.name: scalePositionsToo
param.description: if true, selected object's positions will also be scaled

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetExplicitHandling
function.description: Sets the explicit handling flags for a scene object

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of a scene object.

param.name: explicitFlags
param.description: explicit handling flags. For now only bit 0 is used

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetGraphStreamTransformation
function.description: Applies a transformation to a graph stream

param.name: graphHandle
param.description: handle of the graph

param.name: streamId
param.description: id of the stream

param.name: mult
param.description: multiplication factor

param.name: off
param.description: an offset

param.name: movingAvgPeriod
param.description: moving average period. Set to 1 for no moving average

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetGraphStreamValue
function.description: Sets the next value to be recorded for a graph stream

param.name: graphHandle
param.description: handle of the graph object

param.name: streamId
param.description: id of the stream

param.name: value
param.description: value to set. Omitting to set a value for a corresponding sim_handleGraph call, then the data point will be missing.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetJointDependency
function.description: Sets a joint dependent of another joint. The dependent joint should first be set into dependent mode via sim_setJointMode

param.name: jointHandle
param.description: handle of the joint to become slave

param.name: masterJointHandle
param.description: handle of the joint to become master, or -1 to free the slave joint from dependency

param.name: offset
param.description: offset in equation slave = offset + master * multCoeff

param.name: multCoeff
param.description: coeff in equation slave = offset + master * multCoeff

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetJointInterval
function.description: Sets the interval parameters of a joint (i.e. range values). The attributes or interval parameters might have no effect, depending on the joint-type

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the joint object

param.name: cyclic
param.description: indicates whether the joint is cyclic. Only revolute joints with a lead of 0 can be cyclic

param.name: interval
param.description: joint limits. The first value is the joint lower limit, the second value is the joint range (i.e. the upper limit is lower limit + range)

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetJointMode
function.description: Sets the operation mode of a joint

param.name: jointHandle
param.description: handle of the joint object

param.name: jointMode
param.description: joint mode value

param.name: options
param.description: not used. Set to 0.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetJointPosition
function.description: Sets the linear/angular displacement/angle of a joint. Cannot be used with spherical joints (use sim_setObjectChildPose instead)

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the joint object

param.name: pos
param.description: linear/angular displacement/angle of the joint

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetJointTargetForce
function.description: Sets the force or torque that a joint can exert

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the joint object

param.name: forceOrTorque
param.description: maximum force or torque the joint can exert

param.name: signedValue
param.description: if true, the sign of the force/torque indicates the desired movement direction

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetJointTargetPosition
function.description: Sets the target linear/angular position of a joint. When in kinematic mode, the joint moves according to a motion profile that respects maximum velocity, acceleration and jerk values. In dynamic and position/custom control mode, the controller is instructed about the desired position

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the joint object

param.name: targetPosition
param.description: target position of the joint

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetJointTargetVelocity
function.description: Sets the target linear/angular velocity of a non-spherical joint. When in kinematic mode, the joint moves according to a motion profile that respects maximum acceleration and jerk values. In dynamic and velocity control mode, the controller is instructed about the desired velocity

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the joint object

param.name: targetVelocity
param.description: target velocity of the joint

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetLinkDummy
function.description: Defines (or breaks) a dummy-dummy link pair. Useful to create dynamic loop closure constraints on the fly (among others)

param.name: dummyHandle
param.description: handle of the first dummy in the dummy-dummy link pair

param.name: linkedDummyHandle
param.description: handle of the second dummy in the dummy-dummy link pair. Set to -1 to unlink the first dummy

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetNavigationMode
function.description: Sets the navigation and selection mode for the mouse

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetObjectAlias
function.description: Sets the alias of an object

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object

param.name: objectAlias
param.description: alias of the object. Allowed characters include 0-9, a-z, A-Z, and underscores. Illegal characters are replaced with underscores

param.name: options
param.description: set to 0

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetObjectChildPose
function.description: Can be used to set a spherical joint's rotational transformation (the translational part is ignored)

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object

param.name: pose
param.description: pointer to the pose (array of 7 values [x y z qx qy qz qw])

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetObjectColor
function.description: Sets the color of a scene object

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object

param.name: index
param.description: zero-based index of the color, if the object has several colors

param.name: rgbData
param.description: red, green and blue components of the color (3 values)

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, 0 if the index was not found in the object, 1 otherwise.

function.name: simSetObjectMatrix
function.description: Sets the transformation matrix of an object. Dynamically simulated objects, together with their hierarchy tree, are dynamically reset (this however does not apply to static shapes)

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object. Can be combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe

param.name: relativeToObjectHandle
param.description: indicates relative to which reference frame the matrix is specified. Specify sim_handle_world to set the absolute transformation matrix, sim_handle_inverse to set the inverse of the absolute transformation matrix, sim_handle_parent to set the transformation matrix relative to the object's parent, or an object handle relative to whose reference frame the transformation matrix is specified. If this handle is the handle of a joint, then the matrix is applied relative to the joint's moving frame (unless is combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe, in which case the matrix is applied relative to the joint's base frame)

param.name: matrix
param.description: the matrix (array of 12 values [Vx0 Vy0 Vz0 P0 Vx1 Vy1 Vz1 P1 Vx2 Vy2 Vz2 P2])

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetObjectOrientation
function.description: Sets the orientation ( Euler angles ) of an object. Dynamically simulated objects, together with their hierarchy tree, are dynamically reset (this however does not apply to static shapes)

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object. Can be combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe

param.name: relativeToObjectHandle
param.description: indicates relative to which reference frame the orientation is specified. Specify sim_handle_world to set the absolute orientation, sim_handle_inverse to set the inverse of the absolute orientation, sim_handle_parent to set the orientation relative to the object's parent, or an object handle relative to whose reference frame the orientation is specified. If this handle is the handle of a joint, then the orientation is applied relative to the joint's moving frame (unless is combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe, in which case the orientation is applied relative to the joint's base frame)

param.name: eulerAngles
param.description: Euler angles (alpha, beta and gamma)

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simGetRotationAxis
function.description: Retrieves an axis and rotation angle that brings one pose or transformation matrix onto another one. The translation part of the poses/matrices is ignored. This function, when used in combination with simRotateAroundAxis , can be used to build interpolations between transformation matrices

param.name: matrixStart
param.description:
the
start
transformation matrix (array of 12 values [Vx0 Vy0 Vz0 P0 Vx1 Vy1 Vz1 P1 Vx2 Vy2 Vz2 P2])

param.name: matrixGoal
param.description:
the
goal
transformation matrix (array of 12 values [Vx0 Vy0 Vz0 P0 Vx1 Vy1 Vz1 P1 Vx2 Vy2 Vz2 P2])

param.name: axis
param.description: the returned rotation axis in absolute coordinates (array of 3 values [Vx Vy Vz])

param.name: angle
param.description: the returned rotation angle

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simRotateAroundAxis
function.description: Rotates a pose or transformation matrix around a random axis in space. This function, when used in combination with simGetRotationAxis , can be used to build interpolations between poses or transformation matrices

param.name: matrixIn
param.description: transformation matrix to rotate (array of 12 values [Vx0 Vy0 Vz0 P0 Vx1 Vy1 Vz1 P1 Vx2 Vy2 Vz2 P2])

param.name: axis
param.description: axis vector in absolute coordinates to rotate around

param.name: axisPos
param.description: position of the rotation axis in absolute coordinates

param.name: angle
param.description: amount of rotation to perform

param.name: matrixOut
param.description: returned transformed (rotated) matrix (array of 12 values [Vx0 Vy0 Vz0 P0 Vx1 Vy1 Vz1 P1 Vx2 Vy2 Vz2 P2])

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetObjectParent
function.description: Sets an object's parent object. Dynamically simulated objects, together with their hierarchy tree, are dynamically reset (this however does not apply to static shapes)

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object that will become child of the parent object. Can be or-combined with sim_handleflag_assembly if an assembly operation is desired instead (in that case, can't be -1, and should be set to false)

param.name: parentObjectHandle
param.description: handle of the object that will become parent, or -1 if the object should become parentless. In an assembly operation, the assembly dummy on the parent object should be specified instead

param.name: keepInPlace
param.description: indicates whether the object's absolute pose should stay same

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetObjectPose
function.description: Sets the pose of an object. Dynamically simulated objects, together with their hierarchy tree, are dynamically reset (this however does not apply to static shapes)

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object. Combine with sim_handleflag_wxyzquat to provide the quaternion as [qw qx qy qz] order instead of [qx qy qz qw] order. Can also be combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe

param.name: relativeToObjectHandle
param.description: indicates relative to which reference frame the pose is specified. Specify sim_handle_world to set the absolute pose, sim_handle_inverse to set the inverse of the absolute pose, sim_handle_parent to set the pose relative to the object's parent, or an object handle relative to whose reference frame the pose is specified. If this handle is the handle of a joint, then the pose is applied relative to the joint's moving frame (unless is combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe, in which case the pose is applied relative to the joint's base frame)

param.name: pose
param.description: the pose (array of 7 values [x y z qx qy qz qw])

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetObjectPosition
function.description: Sets the position (x, y and z-coordinates) of an object. Dynamically simulated objects, together with their hierarchy tree, are dynamically reset (this however does not apply to static shapes)

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object. Can be combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe

param.name: relativeToObjectHandle
param.description: indicates relative to which reference frame the position is specified. Specify sim_handle_world to set the absolute position, sim_handle_parent to set the position relative to the object's parent, or an object handle relative to whose reference frame the position is specified. If this handle is the handle of a joint, then the position is applied relative to the joint's moving frame (unless is combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe, in which case the position is applied relative to the joint's base frame)

param.name: position
param.description: coordinates of the object

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetObjectQuaternion
function.description: Sets the quaternion of an object. Dynamically simulated objects, together with their hierarchy tree, are dynamically reset (this however does not apply to static shapes)

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object. Combine with sim_handleflag_wxyzquat to provide the quaternion as [qw qx qy qz] order instead of [qx qy qz qw] order. Can also be combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe

param.name: relativeToObjectHandle
param.description: indicates relative to which reference frame the orientation is specified. Specify sim_handle_world to set the absolute orientation, sim_handle_inverse to set the inverse of the absolute orientation, sim_handle_parent to set the orientation relative to the object's parent, or an object handle relative to whose reference frame the orientation is specified.
