If this handle is the handle of a joint, then the quaternion is applied relative to the joint's moving frame (unless is combined with sim_handleflag_reljointbaseframe, in which case the quaternion is applied relative to the joint's base frame)

param.name: quaternion
param.description: the quaternion (array of 4 values [qx qy qz qw])

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetObjectSel
function.description: Sets the object selection state

param.name: handles
param.description: the handles of objects, or null

param.name: cnt
param.description: the number of objects

param.name: return
param.description: The number of selected objects, or -1 in case of an error

function.name: simSetPage
function.description: Switches between pages (main scene views)

param.name: index
param.description: index of the page. Valid values are 0-7

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetPluginInfo
function.description: Attaches additional information to a loaded plugin

param.name: pluginName
param.description: name of the plugin

param.name: infoType
param.description: the type of information to set

param.name: stringInfo
param.description: string information value, in case the information type is for a string.

param.name: intInfo
param.description: integer information value, in case the information type is for an integer.

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error

function.name: simSetPointCloudOptions
function.description: Sets various properties of a point cloud

param.name: pointCloudHandle
param.description: handle of the point cloud

param.name: maxVoxelSize
param.description: maximum size of the OC tree voxels containing points

param.name: maxPtCntPerVoxel
param.description: maximum number of points allowed in a same OC tree voxel

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): points have random colors
bit1 set (2): show OC tree structure
bit2 set (4): reserved. keep unset
bit3 set (8): do not use an OC tree structure. When enabled, point cloud operations are limited, and point clouds will not be collidable , measurable or detectable anymore, but adding points will be much faster
bit4 set (16): color is emissive

param.name: pointSize
param.description: bit0 set (1): points have random colors

param.name: reserved
param.description: bit1 set (2): show OC tree structure

param.name: return
param.description: 1 if operation was successful

function.name: simSetReferencedHandles
function.description:
Attaches a list of custom handles to a given scene object. Those custom handles are handles of other scene objects, that are linked to the given scene object (for whatever purpose). The advantage of storing references to other objects with this function is that CoppeliaSim will take care of correctly adjusting the references if needed: For instance, imagine
objectA
storing the handle of
objectB
via this function. If
objectB
is deleted, then the stored handle becomes -1. If
objectA
and
objectB
are duplicated at the same time, then the duplicate of
objectA
stores the handle of the duplicate of
objectB
. Optionally, if
sim_handleflag_keeporiginal
is specified, then linking to original objects is guaranteed, e.g. in above example, after a duplication of
objectA
, the duplicate of
objectA
will store the handle of the original
objectB
(if
objectB
still exists)

param.name: objectHandle
param.description:
handle of the scene object that will store the list of handles. Can be optionally combined with
sim_handleflag_keeporiginal
, in which case the handles that link to originals are retrieved

param.name: count
param.description: number of handles to store

param.name: referencedHandles
param.description: list of scene object handles.

param.name: tag
param.description: a tag: handles will be stored under this tag. null defaults to an empty tag

param.name: reserved
param.description: reserved for future extensions

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error.

function.name: simSetShapeColor
function.description: Sets the color of a shape

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape

param.name: colorName
rgbData
param.description: red, green and blue components of the color (3 values), or the transparency value (1 value)

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simSetShapeInertia
function.description: Applies a new inertia matrix to a shape. If simulation is running, the shape is dynamically reset (similar to calling sim_resetDynamicObject right after)

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape object

param.name: inertiaMatrix
param.description:
new inertia matrix (9 values), expressed relative to
transformationMatrix
(itself expressed relative to the shape's reference frame)

param.name: transformationMatrix
param.description:
a transformation matrix (array of 12 values [Vx0 Vy0 Vz0 P0 Vx1 Vy1 Vz1 P1 Vx2 Vy2 Vz2 P2]) expressed relative to the shape's reference frame. The matrix indicates the center of mass of the shape, and is the frame relative to which
inertiaMatrix
is expressed

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error

function.name: simSetShapeMass
function.description: Applies a new mass value to a shape. If simulation is running, the shape is dynamically reset (similar to calling sim_resetDynamicObject right after)

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handle of the shape object

param.name: mass
param.description: new mass of the shape

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error

function.name: simSetShapeTexture
function.description: Applies/removes a texture to/from a shape

param.name: h
param.description: handle of the shape.

param.name: tId
param.description: ID of the texture or -1 to remove any existing texture. See also simGetTextureId , simGetShapeTextureId and simCreateTexture .

param.name: mode
param.description: texture mapping mode .

param.name: opt
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0: if set (1), then adjacent texture pixels are not interpolated.
bit1: if set (2), then the texture is applied as a decal (its appearance won't be influenced by light conditions).
bit2: if set (4), then the texture will be repeated along the U direction.
bit3: if set (8), then the texture will be repeated along the V direction.

param.name: uvScaling
param.description: <pointer to 2 values that indicate the texture scaling factors along the U and V directions

param.name: pos
param.description: pointer to 3 values (x,y,z) that indicate the texture position on the shape. Can be null for default values

param.name: orient
param.description: pointer to 3 values (Euler angles) that indicate the texture orientation on the shape. Can be null for default values

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error.

function.name: simSetVisionSensorImg
function.description: Writes the image of a vision sensor (and applies any image processing via the vision callback functions ). Make sure the vision sensor is flagged as external input

param.name: sensorHandle
param.description: handle of the vision sensor object

param.name: img
param.description: buffer containing the image

param.name: options
param.description:
options, bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): the provided image is a greyscale image (1 byte per pixel), otherwise an RGB image (3 bytes per pixel)
bit1 set (2): the provided image has one additional byte per pixel, as alpha channel (RGBA or greyscale+A)

param.name: pos
param.description: position of the image portion to write. Can be null, which defaults to [0 0]

param.name: size
param.description: size of the image portion to write. Can be null which defaults to [0 0], which corresponds to [resolutionX resolutionY]

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simStartSimulation
function.description: Requests a start of a simulation (or a resume of a paused simulation)

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error, 0 if the operation could not be performed. >0 in case of success.

function.name: simStopSimulation
function.description: Requests a stop of the running simulation

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error, 0 if the operation could not be performed. >0 in case of success.

function.name: simSubtractObjectFromOctree
function.description: Removes an object from an OC tree , as voxel subtractions

param.name: octreeHandle
param.description: handle of the OC tree

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object to subtract. Only potentially collidable objects are supported

param.name: options
param.description: reserved. Set to 0

param.name: reserved
param.description: reserved for future extensions. Set to null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the total number of voxels in the OC tree

function.name: simSubtractObjectFromPointCloud
function.description: Removes an object from a point cloud , as a subtraction

param.name: pointCloudHandle
param.description: handle of the point cloud

param.name: objectHandle
param.description: handle of the object to subtract. Only potentially measurable objects are supported

param.name: options
param.description: reserved. Set to 0

param.name: tolerance
param.description: distance used as a tolerance value

param.name: reserved
param.description: reserved for future extensions. Set to null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful, otherwise the total number of points in the point cloud

function.name: simTransformImage
function.description: Transforms an image in various ways

param.name: image
param.description: pointer to rgb or rgba values of the image

param.name: resolution
param.description: resolution of the image

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 set (1): the provided image is rgba (or a depth buffer, i.e. one double per image pixel), otherwise it is rgb
bit1 set (2): the image will be flipped on its x-axis
bit2 set (4): the image will be flipped on its y-axis

param.name: floatParams
param.description: set to null

param.name: intParams
param.description: set to null

param.name: reserved
param.description: set to null

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful.

function.name: simTransformVector
function.description: Multiplies a vector with a transformation matrix (v = m * v)

param.name: matrix
param.description: the transformation matrix: [Vx0 Vy0 Vz0 P0 Vx1 Vy1 Vz1 P1 Vx2 Vy2 Vz2 P2]

param.name: vect
param.description: the vector to be transformed (a pointer to 3 values)

param.name: return
param.description: -1 if operation was not successful

function.name: simUngroupShape
function.description: Ungroups a compound shape into several shapes

param.name: shapeHandle
param.description: handles of the shape you wish to ungroup. If you specify for this argument (-2-handleOfShape), then the shape will be divided instead of ungrouped

param.name: shapeCount
param.description: (output): size of the returned buffer

param.name: return
param.description: a pointer to an array holding the handles of the resulting shapes, or null in case of an error. The user is in charge of releasing the array buffer with simReleaseBuffer .

function.name: simUnpackTable
function.description: Unpacks a buffer into a table

param.name: stackHandle
param.description: stack handle obtained with simCreateStack . The unpacked table will be pushed onto the stack.

param.name: buffer
param.description: packed table (buffer)

param.name: bufferSize
param.description: size of the buffer

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error.

function.name: simWriteTexture
function.description: Overwrites a specific texture (or a portion of it) with RGB data

param.name: textureId
param.description: ID of the texture. See also simGetTextureId

param.name: options
param.description:
bit-coded:
bit0 reserved. Do not set
bit1 reserved. Do not set
bit2 set (4)=only an elliptical/circular portion of the texture data will be written

param.name: textureData
param.description: RGB data to write onto the texture. Each pixel is represented with 3 bytes (0-255)

param.name: posX
param.description: x/y position where to copy the RGB data. Set to 0/0 to overwrite the full texture

param.name: posY
param.description: x/y position where to copy the RGB data. Set to 0/0 to overwrite the full texture

param.name: sizeX
param.description: x/y size of the RGB data. Set to 0/0 to overwrite the full texture

param.name: sizeY
param.description: x/y size of the RGB data. Set to 0/0 to overwrite the full texture

param.name: interpolation
param.description: fade or interpolation factor. 0.0 for no fade

param.name: return
param.description: -1 in case of an error.
